clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥60 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell precursor or T‑cell lineage. general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Uncontrolled infection.
Positive testing for hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Recent major surgery or radiotherapy within four weeks before study entry.
Symptomatic or untreated leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression.
Active heart disease including New York Heart Association class III‑IV, unstable angina, recent stroke, or myocardial infarction within six months.
Left ventricular ejection fraction less than 40%.
History of another primary invasive malignancy not in complete remission for at least two years (excluding non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ).
Concomitant anticoagulation with warfarin.
Conditions causing inability to take oral medication (e.g., malabsorption syndromes).
Any severe acute or chronic medical or psychiatric disorder judged by the investigator to increase risk or confound assessment.

general_exclusion_criteria: Active or progressing brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease; stable-treated brain metastases permissible under strict criteria. Persistent toxicities from previous systemic antineoplastic treatments greater than Grade 1 (except alopecia and vitiligo). Clinically significant lung disease requiring systemic corticosteroids within six months (interstitial pneumonia, pneumonitis, pulmonary fibrosis, severe radiation pneumonitis). Acute or chronic pancreatitis and/or cirrhosis except Child‑Pugh Class A. Recent hepatic decompensation (jaundice, encephalopathy, variceal bleeding) within sixty days. History of liver transplant. Prior allogenic bone-marrow transplantation. Significant cardiovascular disease (recent MI, ACS, NYHA Class III/IV CHF, uncontrolled hypertension, arrhythmia, severe aortic stenosis). Thromboembolic or cerebrovascular events within three months. Uncontrolled infectious illnesses—including active bacterial, fungal, viral infections; untreated hepatitis B/C or HIV (with exceptions allowing controlled infection meeting predefined virologic/immunologic thresholds). Known or suspected hypersensitivity to study drug components. Participation in another investigational trial concurrently. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Prior malignant neoplasm (other than current indication) within three years, except adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, non‑invasive bladder, cervix, breast, or prostate cancer with no activity for two years. Any other severe acute/chronic medical or psychiatric condition judged unsuitable for enrolment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 18 years. Sex allowed: both male and female. Cancer type allowed: invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease or locally recurrent unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: documented radiographic or objective disease progression on a cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor‑containing regimen in the metastatic setting or relapse/progression occurring during or within twelve months after completion of a CDK4/6 inhibitor‑containing regimen in the adjuvant setting **and** documented disease progression on fulvestrant (alone or as part of a combination) in the metastatic setting **and** continuous gonadotropin‑releasing hormone agonist administration for at least four weeks prior to study entry for pre‑menopausal women and for male participants. Prior treatment excluded: more than two prior chemotherapy regimens in the metastatic setting; any prior megakaryocyte extracellular signal‑regulated kinase (MEK) inhibitor exposure; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib for toxicity (at any dose); prior discontinuation of fulvestrant for toxicity. Biomarkers required: hormone‑receptor positive (estrogen receptor positive **or** progesterone receptor positive) **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative as determined by ASCO/CAP‑qualified laboratory methodology. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: active brain metastases or known carcinomatous meningitis; prior discontinuation of abemaciclib for toxicity (dose‑related restriction applied in phase II); prior discontinuation of fulvestrant for toxicity; prior treatment with any MEK inhibitor; receipt of another investigational agent within thirty days or five half‑lives of first study dose; receipt of chemotherapy within twenty‑one days of first study dose; receipt of endocrine or biologic therapy within fourteen days of first study dose; radiation therapy completed within fourteen days of registration; major surgery performed within fourteen days of registration; pre‑existing ocular pathology—including glaucoma, retinal vein occlusion risk factors, retinal disease, intra‑ocular pressure > 21 mm Hg, corneal epithelial instability, keratitis, or other serious ocular surface inflammation; history of rhabdomyolysis or neuromuscular disorders associated with elevated creatine kinase; uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses such as uncontrolled hypertension, New York Heart Association class III/IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, clinically significant arrhythmias, uncontrolled diabetes, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease, severe chronic liver or kidney disease, or other life‑threatening conditions; history of a second malignancy unless disease‑free ≥five years (except adequately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer or cervical carcinoma in situ, among others as stipulated); hypersensitivity to VS‑6766, abemaciclib, fulvestrant, or excipients thereof; known HIV infection with an AIDS‑defining opportunistic infection within the past twelve months or receiving potent CYP3A4‑interacting antiretrovirals; active hepatitis B or C infection (untreated or inadequately suppressed); exposure to strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers within fourteen days of first dose; pregnant or lactating individuals; inability to swallow oral medication or conditions causing marked malabsorption (gastrectomy, extensive small‑bowel resections, inflammatory bowel disease, baseline diarrhoea grade ≥ 2); active systemic bacterial infection requiring intravenous antibiotics, active systemic fungal infection requiring intravenous antifungals, or symptomatic SARS‑CoV‑2 infection within twenty‑eight days; concomitant warfarin therapy; any other serious or uncontrolled medical condition deeming the individual unsuitable for safe participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumors. Histology allowed: Any solid tumor histology. Prior treatment required: Received at least one prior standard systemic anticancer therapy appropriate for the tumor type (chemotherapy, endocrine therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, or radiotherapy) **or** is intolerant/inadequately served by existing standards when no further approved therapy exists. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA Class II‑IV).
Serious cardiac arrhythmia requiring treatment.
Myocardial infarction or unstable angina within six months preceding enrollment.
Clinically important resting ECG abnormalities (e.g., complete left bundle branch block, third‑degree AV block, second‑degree AV block, PR interval > 250 ms).
Average QTcF > 470 ms in men or women.
Inability or refusal to discontinue concomitant drugs known to prolong the QT interval.
Use of strong CYP3A4 inducing medicines, herbs, or foods that cannot be stopped before first dose.
Past or current non‑infectious interstitial lung disease / pneumonitis or radiation‑induced pneumonitis requiring steroid therapy, or inability to rule out these conditions on screening imaging.
Significant ongoing pulmonary diseases (recent pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, COPD, restrictive lung disease, substantial pleural effusions, autoimmune/connective‐tissue disease with lung involvement, prior total pneumonectomy).
Uncontrolled infection necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy.
Clinically significant corneal disease.
Known HIV infection.
Active viral hepatitis (subject to stipulated virologic limits and lack of suppression therapy as described).
Pregnant or nursing mothers.
Actively progressing brain metastases (untreated, symptomatic, or requiring steroids/con­vulsive prophylaxis); asymptomatic/stable CNS lesions permitted only if off steroids >10 mg prednisolone equivalents daily.
Unresolved adverse events from previous anticancer therapy greater than CTCAE grade 1 (chronic grade 2 toxicities permissible upon sponsor discretion).
More than one primary malignant neoplasm diagnosed within three years prior to enrollment, except appropriately treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, in‑situ carcinomas, superficially invasive urothelial carcinoma, or contralateral breast cancer fully resected.
Substance/alcohol dependence or any condition judged to pose excessive safety risk or impede compliance.
Hypersensitivity to investigational product components or excipients.
Other medically unreasonable factors rendering the participant unsuitable for study conduct as determined by the investigators.

general_exclusion_criteria: inability to swallow oral medications; pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; simultaneous participation in another interventional clinical trial; recent (<2 weeks) systemic anticancer therapy or immunotherapy; recent (<2 weeks) radiotherapy; symptomatic brain metastases requiring >10 mg/day prednisolone equivalents; unresolved adverse effects from prior surgeries, radiotherapctions, or other cancer therapies; history of another malignancy except cured non‑melanoma skin cancer, adequately treated carcinoma in situ, low‑risk localized prostate cancer, or remission ≥1 year; major surgery ≤4 weeks before randomisation; serious active cardiac disease within 6 months ( NYHA class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke ); left ventricular ejection fraction <40%; baseline QTcF ≥450 ms or other risks for torsades/developmental arrhythmias; past or ongoing progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy; uncontrolled infections including HIV or chronic viral hepatitis; inadequate hepatic, renal, or hematologic function; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status >2.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system metastases  
New York Heart Association Class II–IV heart disease, myocardial infarction within 6 months, active ischemia, uncontrolled angina, clinically significant arrhythmia requiring therapy, uncontrolled hypertension, or congestive heart failure  
Use of strong or moderate cyclooxygenase‑3A (CYP3A) enzyme inhibitors or induters, and consumption of grapefruit or grapefruit juice shortly before starting study drug  
Major surgical procedures (e.g., abdominal laparotomy) within 4 weeks preceding first dose of study drug

Gastrointestinal abnormalities that prevent adequate oral absorption (e.g., inability to swallow tablets, severe malabsorption syndrome, dependence on total parenteral nutrition)  
Any active bleeding event excluding mild hemorrhage from hemorrhoids or gums.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with known allergy or hypersensitivity to ivosidenib or venetoclax.
Uncontrolled clinically significant medical conditions, infections, lab abnormalities, or psychiatric illnesses placing the patient at unreasonable risk.
Use of additional chemotherapeutic or anti‑leukemic agents during the study beyond permissible intrathecal chemotherapy, hydroxyurea, or a single low-dose cytarabine course.
Concomitant use of strong CYP3A inducers (e.g., avasimibe, carbamazepine, phenytoin, rifampin, rifabutin, St. John’s Wort) around study start.
Active graft‑versus‑host disease post–stem cell transplantation (unless inactive/chronic suppressed as discussed with investigator).
Severe gastrointestinal or metabolic conditions impairing oral medication absorption.
Concurrent active malignancy requiring ongoing systemic therapy.
Baseline corrected QTcF greater than 450 msec (excluding cases managed after discussion).
Known active hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus infection.
White blood cell count exceeding 25 ×10⁹/L (hydroxyurea permitted to reduce WBC).
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or inadequate contraceptive measures in females of reproductive potential; males must avoid conception-related activities for stipulated duration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: no radiographic evidence of local extension, nodal involvement, or distant metastasis (negative PSMA PET or Axumin scan, pathological stage pN0 or pNx). Prior treatment required: radical prostatectomy (intrafascial or intermfasical) with documented bilateral nerve‑sparring technique. Prior treatment excluded: prior local prostate cancer therapy such as cryotherapy or brachytherapy; prior post‑prostatectomy or pelvic radiation therapy; planned elective pelvic lymph node radiation therapy; prior extensive anorectal surgery leading to loss of rectum. Biomarkers required: PSA >=0.1 ng/mL. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Allergy to hyaluronic acid.
Pathologic T4 disease.
Inflammatory bowel disease requiring steroid treatment (Crohn disease, ulcerative colitis).
Active connective tissue disorder including systemic lupus erythematosus or scleroderma.
Any urogenital anomaly limiting access to the injection site.
White blood cell count <4000/uL or >12000/uL.
Hemoglobin <10 g/dL.
Coagulation abnormalities: activated partial thromboplastin time >35 sec, international normalized ratio >1.4, platelets <100000/mm3.
Serum transaminase levels >2.5× upper limit of normal.
Creatinine >2.0 mg/dL.
Bilirubin >2.0 mg/dL.
History of chronic renal failure.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus (fasting glucose >300 mg/dL).
Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (history of AIDS). Controlled HIV infection permitted if CD4+ count >=350 cells/μL and no AIDS‑defining illnesses.
Contraindication to magnetic resonance imaging or PSMA/PET scanning (e.g., non‑MRI compatible implanted devices).
Presence of bilateral hip prostheses incompatible with MRI artifacts; unilateral MRI‑compatible hip prosthesis allowable if image quality preserved.
Unable or unwilling to comply with study procedures.
Any additional condition deemed by investigators to preclude safe administration or completion of study therapy.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of centrally located neurological metastases that are symptomatic, neurologically unstable, or requiring corticosteroid support.
Concurrent acute kidney injury (developed within 48 hours to 7 days) or chronic kidney disease persisting ≥3 months.
Baseline dry mouth (xerostomia) graded ≥2 per CTCAE version 5.
Uncontrolled hypertension, myocardial infarction, angina pectoris, or coronary artery bypass graft surgery occurring within six months preceding consent, or any actively manifest serious cardiovascular disease.
Previous diagnosis of lymphoproliferative disorder or any other invasive malignancy diagnosed within five years prior to enrollment (excluding adequately treated basal cell skin carcinoma, actinic keratoses without current activity, or resected non‑invasive colorectal polyps).
Use of any investigational therapeutic agent within 28 days prior to randomization.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of distant metastasis outside the abdominopelvic cavity (central nervous system, lungs, bone, etc.).
Recent (within 3 months) bowel obstruction, high risk for bowel obstruction, need for total parental nutrition, or dependency on IV fluids.
Solid organ transplant recipient.
Active or progressive additional malignancy, or history of another malignancy within 2 years (exceptions noted).
Severe or anaphylactic allergy to substances chemically or biologically similar to IL‑2/N‑803 or to murine‑based antibodies.
Immune‑related toxicity from prior checkpoint inhibitor resulting in permanent cessation or requiring intense/prolonged immunosuppression (unless controlled endocrine irAE).
Autoimmune disorders: inflammatory bowel disease (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease), rheumatic arthritis, systemic scleroderma, systemic lupus erythematosus, ANCA-associated vasculitis, Guillain‑Barré syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis (Hashimoto thyroiditis permitted).
Chronic systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone-equivalent for ≥4 weeks before infusion.
Uncontrolled comorbidities: active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias, serious psychiatric disorder limiting protocol adherence.
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for women of childbearing potential and males.
HIV‑positive status.
Active untreated hepatitis B or C infection.
Known non‑infectious pneumonitis or any interstitial lung disease.
Receipt of a live attenuated vaccine within 30 days of study initiation.
Grade 2 or greater hemolytic anemia documented historically.
Any condition judged by investigators to increase risk of mortality or compromise safety/effectiveness assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18-84 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: ovary. Histology allowed: high grade serous ovarian carcinoma and high grade endometrioid ovarian carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent disease confined to the abdomen or pelvis with no extra‑abdominal metastases and classified as platinum‑responsive or platinum‑tolerant (no requirement for platinum resistance). Prior treatment required: at least one prior systemic anticancer therapy. Prior treatment excluded: concurrent administration of any other investigational agents. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: germline BRCA1 mutation or germline BRCA2 mutation or somatic BRCA1 mutation or somatic BRCA2 mutation.

general_exclusion_criteria: Patients with unresolved Grade >1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia); receipt of palliative radiotherapy within 14 days or definitive radiotherapy within 42 days of Cycle 1 Day 1 without resolution to baseline; participation in another investigational study concurrently; hypersensitivity to components of peposertab or liposomal doxorubicin; inability to discontinue strong CYP3A4/5, CYP2C9, or CYP2C19 modulators as stipulated; inability to discontinue proton‐pump inhibitors appropriately; left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s lower limits of normal; uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness; inability to swallow oral capsules whole; active progressing brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease; pregnancy or lactation; and any condition judged by investigators to compromise safety or compliance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: metastatic invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer. Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally advanced or distant metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: progressed on 0–1 prior systemic therapy lines in the metastatic setting (includes any prior chemotherapy; for hormone‐receptor‑positive disease requires prior endocrine therapy with progression within 12 months of adjuvant end or ≥1 line in metastatic setting **and** prior CDK4/6 inhibitor). Prior treatment excluded: any prior topoisomerase I inhibitor‑containing regimen; prior trastuzumab deruxtecan (when assigned to datopotamab deruxtecan arm) or vice versa (not applicable here). Biomarkers required: HER2‑low (IHC 1+ or IHC 2+ / ISH‑negative) **or** HER2‑zero (IHC 0) on all tested specimens. Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive (IHC 3+ or ISH‑amplified).
general_exclusion_criteria: Concurrent use of other investigational anti‑cancer agents. Prior/topoisomerase I‑targeting ADCs beyond stipulated exceptions. Live attenuated vaccination within 30 days. Significant corneal disease. Severe hypersensitivity to study drugs or other monoclonal antibodies. Recent major surgery (within 2 weeks). Uncontrolled comorbidities or infections, including active pneumonia, sepsis, untreated HIV/HBV/HCV, or active TB. Current or unresolved pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease. Underlying serious pulmonary diseases (severe COPD, asthma, pulmonary fibrosis, recent PE, pneumonectomy). Prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms women, >450 ms men). Cardiovascular events within 6 months (CABG, MI, unstable angina, CHF NYHA II‑III, stroke). Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use effective contraception. Poor functional status (ECOG ≥ 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: renal cell carcinoma. Histology allowed: clear cell renal cell carcinoma (includes tumours with sarcomatoid differentiation). Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: at least one immune checkpoint inhibitor and at least one tyrosine kinase inhibitor (administered in neoadjuvant, adjuvant, or metastatic setting). Prior treatment excluded: prior PSMA‑targeted therapy (e.g., ¹⁷⁷Lu‑PSMA‑617). Biomarkers required: PSMA‑positive lesion with uptake greater than normal liver on ³⁴⁸Ga‑PSMA‑11 PET/CT and no PSMA‑negative lesions larger than 1 cm. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of untreated brain metastases; symptomatic spinal cord compression; persistent grade >1 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia, sensory neuropathy up to grade 2 allowable); hypersensitivity reaction to substances chemically related to ¹⁷⁷Lu‑PSMA‑617; concurrent active malignancy expected to confound safety/efficacy assessments (excluding certain low‑risk cured cancers); significant uncontrolled medical conditions such as interstitial lung disease, severe resting dyspnea requiring supplemental O₂, severe renal insufficiency (estimated GFR <30 mL/min), inflammatory bowel disease leading to baseline grade ≥2 diarrhea; pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: prostate cancer. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior gonadotropin‑releasing hormone agonist or antagonist therapy **and** second‑generation androgen receptor‑targeted therapy (abiraterone, enzalutamide, darolutamide, apalutamide) **and** other investigational agents. Biomarkers required: serum testosterone >200 ng/mL prior to initiation of androgen deprivation therapy. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: history of major adverse cardiac event (myocardial infarction, new or worsening congestive heart failure, or stroke) within the past six months; presence of brain metastases; documented allergic reaction to leuprolide depot or relugolix or structurally similar substances; uncontrolled intercurrent medical illnesses; inability to swallow oral medications.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: Chronic lymphocytic leukemia cells (B‑cell lineage); no history of prolymphocytic leukemia or Richter’s transformation. Cancer burden allowed: Disease meets established criteria indicating need for treatment; at least one measurable lesion on CT or MRI. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Unable to comply with protocol requirements.
Active hepatitis B virus infection.
Active hepatitis C virus infection.
Positive human immunodeficiency virus serology (unless predefined exceptions are met).
Major surgical procedure performed ≤ 28 days before first dose of study drug.
Uncontrolled autoimmune hemolytic anemia or immune thrombocytopenia requiring treatment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years or older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Adenoid cystic carcinoma of the salivary glands (head and neck region). Histology allowed: Adenoid cystic carcinoma (including cases where fine‑needle aspirates show features suggesting adenoid cystic carcinoma or a basaloid neoplasm). Cancer burden allowed: Localized primary tumor suitable for definitive surgical management, no evidence of recurrent disease, no distant metastasis, measurable lesion ≥1 cm × 1 cm. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of other investigational agents. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including, but not limited to, ongoing or active infection, symptomatic congestive heartfailure, unstable angina pectoris, cardiac arrhythmia, or psychiatric illness/social situation compromising safety of biopsy/surgery.
Pregnant women.
Uncorrectable bleeding or coagulation disorders increasing procedural risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumor. Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: advanced, metastatic, or unresectable disease with at least one measurable lesion according to RECIST version 1.1. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior B7H3‑targeted therapy; prior antibody‑drug conjugates with a topoisomerase I inhibitor payload (applies to Cohort B and Phase 1b expansion). Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of spinal cord compression.
Active leptomeningeal disease or uncontrolled/untreated brain metastasis.
Any additional malignant neoplasm diagnosed within 2 years preceding enrollment, excluding adequately treated localized skin cancers, superficial bladder cancer, cervical intraepithelial neoplasia, or carcinoma in situ of the breast.
History of interstitial lung disease, grade 2 or higher non‑infectious pneumonitis, resting arterial oxygen saturation less than 92 %, or requirement for supplemental oxygen at baseline.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Grade greater than 1 persistent abnormalities in serum potassium, sodium, or corrected calcium despite optimal medical management.
Recent serious infection (e.g., bacterial, fungal, viral including tuberculosis) requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukemia. Histology allowed: BCR‑ABL1 positive (Philadelphia chromosome positive) B‑cell lineage acute lymphoblastic leukemia (CD19+). Cancer burden allowed: Diagnosis of acute leukemia fulfilling WHO criteria (blasts ≥20 % in marrow/peripheral blood) irrespective of prior response; asymptomatic central nervous system disease permissible. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior treatment with dasatinib or asciminib. Biomarkers required: BCR‑ABL1 fusion identified by FISH, conventional karyotyping, or RT‑PCR detecting p210 (b2a2/b3a2) or p190 (e1a2) transcripts; CD19 surface expression demonstrated. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of ABL T315I mutation.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe cardiac comorbidity (heart failure or cardiomyopathy with left ventricular ejection fraction ≤50%).  
Severe pulmonary comorbidity (DLCO ≤65 % predicted, FEV₁ ≤65 % predicted, resting dyspnea, or dependence on supplemental oxygen).  
ECOG performance status greater than allowable limits (generally >3).  
Uncorrected prolonged QTc (>480 ms) on baseline ECG.  
Major surgery within 2 weeks preceding first dose.  
Active uncontrolled infection.  
Unstable cardiovascular conditions (symptomatic congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction or cerebrovascular accident within 3 months, clinically significant arrhythmias).  
Significant concomitant illnesses impairing ability to tolerate study drugs (gastrointestinal disorders affecting absorption, hepatic impairment exceeding stipulated laboratory thresholds, renal insufficiency with creatinine >1.5× ULN).  
Known active hepatitis B or C infection, or other chronic liver disease.  
Human immunodeficiency virus infection.  
Pulmonary arterial hypertension.  
Clinically significant pleural effusion requiring drainage.  

Alcohol misuse requiring medical management.  
Pancreatitis (acute or chronic) or ongoing pancreatic disease.  
Bleeding diatheses unrelated to leukemia.  
Concurrent use of prohibited interacting medicines (strong CYP3A4/5 inducers or inhibitors, narrow‑therapeutic‑index CYP substrate drugs, proton pump inhibitors/H₂ blockers, grapefruit products).  
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for individuals of child‑bearing potential.  
Inability to swallow oral medication.  
Any other condition that, in the investigator’s judgment, compromises participant safety or data integrity.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: KMT2A rearranged **or** NUP98 rearranged **or** NPM1 mutated. Cancer burden allowed: First or higher relapse with bone marrow infiltration (single marrow specimen showing ≥5 % leukemic blasts by flow cytometry/FISH/molecular methods) **or** refractory disease defined by bone marrow containing ≥1 % leukemic blasts after two cycles of induction (**or** peripheral blood absolute blast count ≥1,000 cells/µL when marrow unavailable); isolates extramedullary disease, isolated CNS relapse, symptomatic CNS III disease, and acute promyelocytic leukemia are excluded. Prior treatment required: Previously administered conventional chemotherapy for acute leukemia (including prior induction protocols and/or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Central‑laboratory confirmed KMT2A rearrangement **or** NUP98 rearrangement **or** NPM1 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Participants unlikely to comply with study requirements.; Individuals with Down syndrome.; Patients with isolated extramedullary disease (extramedullary relapse) or isolated central nervous system relapse or symptomatic CNS III disease.; History of acute promyelocytic leukemia or juvenile myelomonocytic leukemia.; Malabsorption syndromes prohibiting oral medication intake.; Concurrent use of proton pump inhibitors (alternative H₂ blockers permissible).; Current participation in another investigational drug study.; Known inherited bone‑marrow failure syndromes.; Documented allergy to any protocol medicines.; Active, uncontrolled systemic infection at screening.; Uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, Hepatitis B virus infection, or Hepatitis C virus infection.; Positive pregnancy test in post‑menarchal women or breastfeeding mothers.; Conditions predisposing to severe infection (e.g., cystic fibrosis, immune deficiencies, chronic bleeding disorders, unexplained cytopenias).; Receipt of other investigational agents within 30 days preceding study drug initiation.; Clinically significant congenital cardiovascular abnormalities (e.g., long QT syndrome, unrepaired major structural defects).; Medical conditions judged by investigators to render study treatment unsafe or confound toxicity evaluation.; Hypersensitivity to fludarabine or cytarabine or their excipients.; Live vaccination within six months prior to enrollment.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 0-21 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Acute lymphoblastic leukemia, mixed‑phenotype acute leukemia, or acute undifferentiated leukemia. Histology allowed: KMT2A rearranged **or** NUP98 rearranged **or** NPM1 mutated. Cancer burden allowed: First or higher relapse with bone marrow infiltration (single marrow specimen showing ≥5 % leukemic blasts by flow cytometry/FISH/molecular methods) **or** refractory disease defined by bone marrow containing ≥1 % leukemic blasts at the end of induction/consolidation **or** persistent measurable residual disease (>0.01 %) prior to hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (**or** peripheral blood absolute blast count ≥1,000 cells/µL when marrow unavailable); isolates extramedullary disease, isolated CNS relapse, symptomatic CNS III disease, and acute promyelocytic leukemia are excluded. Prior treatment required: Previously administered conventional chemotherapy for acute leukemia (including prior induction protocols and/or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Central‑laboratory confirmed KMT2A rearrangement **or** NUP98 rearrangement **or** NPM1 mutation. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: at least one active and measurable brain metastasis (measurable per RECIST v1.1, longest diameter ≥10 mm). Prior treatment required: at least one endocrine therapy. Prior treatment excluded: abemaciclib administered in the metastatic setting; elacestrant or any other investigative selective estrogen receptor degrader (SERD), selective estrogen receptor modulator (SERM), SERCAN, CERAN, or PROTAC agent in the metastatic setting; receipt of more than two chemotherapy regimens; receipt of more than two prior cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 (CDK 4/6) inhibitor regimens. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor‐positive (≥1 % tumour cells stained by IHC) and human epidermal growth factor receptor‑2‑negative (IHC 0‑1+ or in‑situ hybridisation negative), assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Immediate central nervous system‑specific treatment judged urgently required by the treating physician.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of grade ≥ 2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy that have not resolved to ≤grade 1 (unless medically acceptable).
Concurrent use of other investigational agents.
Clinically uncontrolled brain metastases or meninges involvement.
Severe hypersensitivity reaction (Grade ≥ 3) to avelumab, M1774, or similar biologics; uncontrolled asthma.
Active untreated systemic infection.
Uncontrolled autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression (except select endocrine disorders managed without immunosuppression).
Previous solid-organ or hematopoietic stem‐cell transplantation.
Significant gastrointestinal pathology (bowel obstruction, uncontrolled diarrhoea, inflammatory bowel disease flare).
Psychiatric illness or social situation judged unsuitable for compliance.
Cardiac instability (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, serious arrhythmias, recent MI, unstable angina, cerebrovascular accident/TIA within 180 days).
Substance/alcohol dependence.
Other malignant neoplasm unless disease free ≥5 years (exceptions: carcinoma in situ of breast/cervix, basal/squamous skin cancer within 5 years).
Recent live vaccine exposure (≤4 weeks) or experimental vaccinations (only inactivated vaccines permissible).
Use of prohibited concomitant medicines (strong CYP3A4/CYP1A2 modulators, certain PPIs, herbal supplements) within 7 days of starting protocol therapy.
Pregnancy or lactation; inadequate contraceptive measures for women of childbearing potential.
Baseline corrected QT interval >470 msec persisting despite electrolytes optimization.

Pregnancy or breastfeeding; unwillingness to use effective contraception.

history of non‑infectious pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease requiring steroids or active pneumonitis/ILD; untreated, symptomatic, or recently operated intracranial metastases (brain metastases requiring therapy, or surgically addressed within 1 month); dependence on percutaneous endoscopic gastrojejunostomy tube or total parental nutrition; receipt of any investigational product or device within 4 weeks preceding registration; receipt of live or live‑attenuated vaccination within 30 days (killed vaccines permitted); additional malignancies that are progressing or have required active treatment within the past 3 years (exception for certain skin carcinomas and low‑risk localized prostate cancer); concurrent use of contraindicated medicines influencing CYP3A4/5 or P‑glycoprotein pathways; uncontrolled comorbid conditions—including active infection, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (within 6 months), serious cardiac arrhythmias, poorly controlled diabetes, recent peptic ulcer disease, chronic liver disease, chronic kidney disease, severe malnutrition; psychiatric disorder or psychosocial circumstances compromising adherence; persistent refractory nausea/vomiting, inflammatory gastrointestinal disease, extensive bowel resections preventing adequate oral drug absorption; baseline corrected QT interval ≥470 ms or documented congenital long QT/QTp syndromes, or use of medications known to markedly lengthen QT; active tuberculosis necessitating systemic therapy; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppression within the past two years (aside from physiological hormonal replacements); immunodeficiency or systemic glucocorticoids greater than 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents within 7 days; inability to avoid pregnancy or breastfeeding—or intention to conceive or sire offspring—from screening through 120 days after the last study dose; prior allogeneic solid organ or bone‑marrow transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: any. Prior treatment excluded: prior systemic chemotherapy directed at metastatic disease; prior EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy beyond the allowances mentioned. Biomarkers required: activating EGFR somatic mutation in tumour tissue or cfDNA and detectable plasma EGFR mutation at Cycle 2 Day 1 (screening assay). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Pregnancy or breastfeeding  
History of clinically significant interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis  
Active uncontrolled central nervous system metastases  
Unresolved adverse events higher than CTCAE grade 1 from prior therapies (except alopecia and grade 2 peripheral sensory neuropathy due to prior platinums)  
Significant cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, or mean QTc > 470 ms  
Baseline electrocardiographic abnormalities requiring intervention (e.g., complete left bundle branch block, third-degree AV block)  
Electrolyte disturbances affecting cardiac repolarization (hypokalaemia, hypomagnesaemia, hypo­calcaemia)  
Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inducers that cannot be stopped  
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh Class C) or transaminases >5 ×ULN irrespective of liver metastasis  
Renal insufficiency with creatinine clearance <60 mL/min  
Active infection with hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or human immunodeficiency virus

Clinically significant abnormality in hematologic, hepatic, or renal laboratory values. Active serious infection or immunodeficiency (including untreated HIV, hepatitis B or C). Uncontrolled cardiac conditions (heart failure, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months). Interstitial lung disease or prior pneumonitis. Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals. Any condition judged by investigator to interfere with safe completion of the study.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate adenocarcinoma. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma of the prostate without small cell differentiation. Cancer burden allowed: High‑risk localized disease defined by PSA ≥20 ng/mL and/or Gleason sum >8, or predominance of Gleason pattern 4 in ≥6 systematic cores (dominant pattern 4 ≥50%), or Gleason pattern 4 dominance in ≥3 cores with PSA ≥20, or ≥25% Gleason pattern 5 in ≥3 cores; additionally permits oligometastatic disease with fewer than three lesions detected solely by PSMA PET when surgical removal is feasible. Prior treatment required: No prior definitive systemic or locoregional therapy for prostate cancer (up to 30 days of leutinizing‑hormone releasing hormone agonist permitted). Prior treatment excluded: Androgen‐deprivation therapy exceeding 30 days, chemotherapy, prostate surgery, external beam radiation, brachytherapy, or any hormonal agent (androgens, estrogens, progestins) administered concurrently or within six months preceding randomisation. Biomarkers required: Provision of fresh or archival tumor tissue suitable for next‑generation sequencing/genomic profiling; informed consent for genetic testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Stage T4 prostate cancer by exam or imaging.
Hypogonadism/severe androgen deficiency (testosterone >50 ng/dL below institutional lower limit).
Active infection or chronic liver disease requiring systemic therapy.
Human immunodeficiency virus infection with detectable viral load.
Uncontrolled or recently significant cardiovascular disease—including myocardial infarction, unstable angina, recent coronary intervention, New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, or major arrhythmia within the past year.
Uncontrolled hypertension.
Inability to swallow oral medications or gastrointestinal disorders markedly impairing drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease, untreated nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive intestinal resections).
Known hypersensitivity to any investigational product components.
History of non‑compliance with prescribed regimens.
Severe concurrent illness rendering the subject unsuitable for radical prostatectomy.
Metastatic disease classified as M1 by conventional cross‑sectional imaging (CT, bone scintigraphy).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: NA. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: NA. Prior treatment required: Continuous oral glucocorticoid therapy (prednisone or prednisolone) at a stable dose of 15–45 mg/day (patients stabilized on 10–14 mg/day together with another non‑steroidal anti‑inflammatory agent may qualify if dose is increased to 15–45 mg/day before randomisation). Prior treatment excluded: Allogeneic haematopoietic stem cell transplantation; solid organ transplantation (any organ except corneal); systemic glucocorticoids used for diseases other than VEXAS that would impede steroid taper or efficacy evaluation; more than one intensive‑care‑unit admission for a VEXAS flare in the six months before enrolment; receipt of nine or more units of packed red blood cells in the ninety days before enrolment; established myelodysplastic syndrome that requires antineoplastic therapy, has undergone allogeneic transplantation, or is categorised as high‑risk or very‑high‑risk according to the revised International Prognostic Scoring System; any invasive malignancy diagnosed within twelve months before enrolment (exceptions: fully treated non‑melanoma skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or qualified myelodysplastic syndromes/multiple‑myeloma precursors such as MGUS or smouldering MM); exposure to a hypomethylating agent within six months before enrolment or accumulation of more than four total cycles of any hypomethylating agent at any time; use of non‑glucocorticoid anti‑inflammatory medications or haematinic/supportive therapies within disallowed intervals prior to enrolment; regular intake of anti‑platelet medicines other than low‑dose aspirin (≤100 mg per day); administration of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inducers within five elimination half‑lives before enrolment; clinically significant bleeding graded CTCAE ≥2 occurring within three months before enrolment (unless clearly attributable to a discrete incident); medically meaningful cardiovascular disease—including symptomatic heart failure limiting usual activities—or electrocardiographic abnormalities/completed QTcF > 480 msec measured within thirty days before enrolement; arterial, venous or cerebral thromboembolic episodes within sixty days before enrolment; moderate or severe hepatic insufficiency corresponding to Child‑Pugh class B or C, or active viral hepatitis; uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection (off antiretroviral therapy or with detectable viraemia despite therapy); positive quantitative interferon‑gamma release assay indicating latent tuberculosis; proven disseminated mycobacterial infection; simultaneous participation in another interventional research programme or receipt of an experimental medicinal product within twenty‑eight days (or five half‑lifes periods, whichever is greater) before enrolment; current pregnancy, intent to become pregnant during the study, or nursing postpartum; active acute bacterial, fungal or viral infection necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy at screening (prophylaxis or chronic suppressive regimens permitted); known allergy to pacritinib or excipients (microcrystalline cellulose, polyethylen​e glycol, magnesium stearate). Biomarkers required: Presence of a pathogenic somatic mutation in the UBA1 gene located at codon methionine‑41 or immediately adjoining splice‑site regions (c.118‑1, c.118‑2) identified using validated next‑generation sequencing, droplet‑digital PCR or Sanger methodology performed on peripheral blood or bone‑marrow specimens. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis; congestive heart failure causing functional limitation; impaired renal function (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min); marked hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >4× ULN, transaminases >3× ULN); uncontrolled central nervous system/metastatic disease involving the brain; unresolved HIV infection or active hepatitis B/C; Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group/Karnofsky performance status less than requisite threshold.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis.
Unresolved serious adverse events from prior anticancer therapy (grade > 2 persisting toxicities).
Imminent or established spinal cord compression.
Active central nervous system involvement (untreated brain metastases or leptomeningeal disease) unless appropriately stabilized post‑radiation or surgery.
Recent (<6 months) myocardial infarction, uncontrolled angina, or symptomatic congestive heart failure (NYHA class III–IV).
Corrected QT interval ≥470 ms or factors prolonging QTc.
Uncontrolled arterial hypertension (SBP > 160 mm Hg or DBP > 100 mm Hg).
Significant infectious illness requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Moderate-to-severe hepatic impairment (Child‑Pugh class C) or total bilirubin >1.5× upper limit of normal.
Renal impairment with creatinine clearance <30 ml/min.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2.
Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial.

Active pregnancy or lactation.

Untreated or inadequately controlled hepatitis C infection (detectable viral load above quantitative threshold)."

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse glioma (inclusive of diffuse astrocytoma, oligodendroglioma, glioblastoma). Histology allowed: Diffuse astrocytoma OR Oligodendroglioma OR Glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: First radiographic relapse after prior therapy. general_exclusion_criteria:
1. Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial.
2. Concurrent treatment with other investigational anticancer agents or devices, including carmustine wafer implants and proteasome inhibitors at recurrence.
3. Prior therapy with procarbazine, lomustine, vincristine (components of PCV), carmustine wafer implants, or bevacizumab.
4. Prior treatment with poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase (PARP) inhibitors.
5. Major surgery (other than surgery for recurrent diffuse glioma) within four weeks prior to study treatment.
6. Radiotherapy delivered within twelve weeks prior to documentation of progression, unless progression occurs entirely outside the irradiated volume or is pathologically verified.
7. History of cranial radiotherapy exceeding 65 Gray, stereotactic radiosurgery, or brachytherapy, unless recurrence is histologically confirmed.
8. Requirement for full‑dose anticoagulation unless coagulation parameters are therapeutically controlled and dosing has been stable for at least two weeks.
9. Concomitant use of strong CYP2D6 or CYP2C19 substrate medicines that cannot be substituted.
10. Use of enzyme‑inducing antiepileptic drugs (EIAEDs) without switching to non‑inducing alternatives at least two weeks before enrollment.
11. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
12. Known hypersensitivity to any component of NMS‑03305293 or temozolomide formulation.
13. Active bacterial, fungal, viral (including HIV-positive) infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.
14. Prolonged corrected QT interval (QTc ≥460 ms for females, ≥450 ms for males) or significant risk factors for torsades de pointe.
15. Clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (e.g., ulcers, inflammatory bowel disease, short-gut syndrome).
16. Recent cardiovascular events within six months (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, coronary or peripheral arterial bypass, symptomatic congestive heart failure, stroke, or transient ischemic attack) or active bleeding diathesis.
17. Prior invasive malignant neoplasm (aside from adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer, carcinoma in situ, or localized cancer disease‑free for ≥3 years).
18. Severe acute or chronic medical, psychiatric, or laboratory abnormalities judged by investigator to pose undue risk or confound outcome assessments.

general_exclusion_criteria: Condition that would confound assessment of safety/efficacy, known active or past central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma, history of pneumonitis, congestive heart failure, significant renal impairment, clinically significant hepatic dysfunction, untreated/uncontrolled brain metastases, human immunodeficiency virus infection, chronic viral hepatitis infection, inadequate organ function, pregnancy or breastfeeding, concurrent participation in another interventional investigational drug study.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: Malignant melanoma of cutaneous origin (acral included, uveal and mucosal excluded). Cancer burden allowed: Resectable stage III disease (AJCC 8th edition TX‐to‐T4 with N1b/N1c/N2b/N2c/N3b/N3c) **or** oligometastatic stage IV disease confined to lungs (M1a or M1b) that is surgically resectable at baseline. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anti‑PD‑1 therapy (nivolumab, pembrolizumab), anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑ID​O, anti‑CD137 agents or any combination containing these; systemic corticosteroids >10 mg prednisone equivalent daily or other systemic immunosuppressants used for active autoimmune disease. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of interstitial lung disease or active non‑infectious pneumonitis.
Active (symptomatic or progressive) central nervous system or leptomeningeal metastases.
Unresolved grade ≥3 immune‑related adverse event from prior immunotherapy (unless fully resolved to grade ≤1).
Concurrent serious medical illnesses (significant cardiac, hepatic, renal impairment) judged unacceptable by investigators.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/father a child during the study period.
Positive serologic tests for human immunodeficiency virus, untreated hepatitis B virus, or untreated hepatitis C virus infection.
Recent administration of live attenuated vaccines (within 30 days before starting study medication).
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to dostarlimab, cobolimab, interferon‑α, or ipilimumab.
Other active malignancy requiring systemic therapy (exceptions: adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas, in‑situ cervical cancer).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease; ineligible for or failed autologous stem‑cell transplant; post‑chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy failure or ineligible for CAR‑T. Prior treatment required: Failure of at least one prior line of systemic antitumour therapy and meets the transplant and CAR‑T criteria noted above. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Fresh or archived tumour tissue specimen not previously irradiated available for assessment. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Solid organ transplant recipients.
Diagnosis of primary mediastinal B‑cell lymphoma.
Clinically significant active cardiovascular disease or serious cardiac arrhythmias requiring medication.
Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease of any grade or receiving treatment for GVHD.
Clinically significant pericardial or pleural effusions.
Grade greater than 1 peripheral neuropathy persisting at baseline.
History of a second malignancy unless treated curatively with no evidence of disease for at least two years.
Demyelinating forms of Charcot‑Marie‑tooth disease.
Contraindications to any study-intervention components, including prior anaphylactic reactions.
Active central nervous system lymphoma involvement (unless in radiographic, cytologic and clinical remission).
Active infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy.
Known Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
Known active Hepatitis C viral infection.
Psychiatric or substance‑abuse disorder impairing participation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Active central nervous system metastases.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness, including cardiovascular disease.
Mean corrected QT interval greater than 470 milliseconds.
Left ventricular ejection fraction lower than 50%.
Major surgical procedures performed within two weeks prior to starting study treatment.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Gastrointestinal conditions that would impair oral administration of study drugs.
Actively ongoing bleeding (excluding minor hemorrhage such as hemorrhoids or gingival bleeding).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma (ductal, lobular, or other). Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable disease (treated or stable brain metastases permitted). Prior treatment required: Receipt of a poly‑ADP‑ribose‐polymerase inhibitor (PARP inhibitor). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation in BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANCE, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN, RAD50, or RAD51B identified by a CLIA‑certified assay (germline or somatic). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation therapy administered within 2 weeks (6 weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C).  
• Unresolved toxicity greater than Grade 1 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).  
• Concurrent participation in another investigational therapeutic protocol.  
• Known hypersensitivity to novobiocin or chemically related compounds.  
• Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers, or medicines that are substrates of BCRP/ABC​G2, OATP1B1/B3/2B1, or OAT1/OAT3 within 14 days of starting study drug.  
• Intake of herbal or dietary supplements with recognized hepatotoxic potential.  
• Current use of medications that carry a substantial risk of QT‑interval prolongation or torsades de pointe (unless limited to granisetron; ondansetron alone is allowed provided no additional QT‑risk agents are taken).  
• Homozygosity for UGT1A1*28, UGT1A1*6, or compound heterozygosity for *28/**6 leading to markedly reduced enzyme activity.  
• Active, uncontrolled comorbid illness—including unstable cardiovascular disease beyond New York Heart Association functional class 2B, uncontrolled hypertension, serious infections, or clinically significant pulmonary, hepatic (acute liver injury, Child‑Pugh C cirrhosis), or renal insufficiency (glomerular filtration rate < 60 mL/min).  
• Untreated or inadequately managed substance abuse disorders, including active alcoholism.  
• Pregnancy or current lactation; women of reproductive potential and males must practice adequate contraception throughout the study and for 4 months thereafter.  
• Inability to comprehend the study’s nature or to execute the signed informed‑consent form.

Biomarkers required: Pathogenic mutation in BRCA1, BRCA2, PALB2, RAD51C, RAD51D, ATM, BARD1, BLM, BRIP1, CDK12, FANCA, FANCC, FANCD2, FANGE, FANCF, FANCM, MRE11A, NBN, RAD50, or RAD51B identified by a CLIA‑certified assay (germline or somatic). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Recent cytotoxic chemotherapy or radiation therapy administered within 2 weeks (6 weeks for nitrosoureas or mitomycin C).  
• Unresolved toxicity greater than Grade 1 attributable to prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).  
• Concurrent participation in another investigational therapeutic protocol.  
• Known hypersensitivity to novobiocin or chemically related compounds.  
• Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4/5 inhibitors or inducers, or medicines that are substrates of BCRP/ABC​G2, OATP1B1/B3/2B1, or OAT1/OAT3 within 14 days of starting study drug.  
• Intake of herbal or dietary supplements with recognized hepatotoxic potential.  
• Current use of medications that carry a substantial risk of QT‑interval prolongation or torsades de pointe (unless limited to granisetron; ondansetron alone is allowed provided no additional QT‑risk agents are taken).  
• Homozygosity for UGT1A1*28, UGT1A1*6, or compound heterozygosity for *28/**6 leading to markedly reduced enzyme activity.  
• Active, uncontrolled comorbid illness—including unstable cardiovascular disease beyond New York Heart Association functional class 2B, uncontrolled hypertension, serious infections, or clinically significant pulmonary, hepatic (acute liver injury, Child‑Pugh C cirrhosis), or renal insufficiency (glomerular filtration rate < 60 mL/min).  
• Untreated or inadequately managed substance abuse disorders, including active alcoholism.  
• Pregnancy or current lactation; women of reproductive potential and males must practice adequate contraception throughout the study and for 4 months thereafter.  
• Inability to comprehend the study’s nature or to execute the signed informed‑consent form.

Age range allowed: 1 to 30 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma. Histology allowed: neuroblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory high‑risk disease (relapse after completing frontline high‑risk therapy, progression during frontline therapy, or primary refractory disease after ≥4 cycles induction) **AND** presence of evaluatable/measurable disease (tumor ≥10 mm on imaging, or MIBG‑avid lesion, or FDG‑avid lesion); isolated bone‑marrow‐only disease prohibited. Prior treatment required: completion of frontline high‑risk neuroblastoma therapy (multidrug induction +/- consolidations such as surgery, radiation, autologous stem‑cell rescue). Prior treatment excluded: prior naxitamab; prior discontinuation of irinotecan or temozolomide owing to unacceptable toxicity; prior discontinuation of GD₂‑directed immunotherapy due to unacceptable toxicity (except allergic reactions). Biomarkers required: elevated urinary catecholamines greater than twice the upper limit of normal (or documentation of neuroblastoma cells in bone marrow accompanied by such catecholamine excess). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pneumonitis  
New York Heart Association Class III–IV congestive heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction <40%  
Estimated glomerular filtration rate markedly reduced (below thresholds defining adequate renal function)  
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >1.5 × upper limit of normal or aminotransferases >5 × ULN)  
Active, untreated central nervous system metastatic disease  
Known active HIV infection or chronic hepatitis B or C requiring antiviral therapy  
Performance status poorer than Karnofsky 70% (adults) or Lansky 70% (children)

* Life expectancy less than 60 days or any concomitant life‑threatening illness, organ dysfunction, or medical condition placing participant at unacceptable risk.

Uncontrolled intercurrent illnesses, including active infections or conditions judged unsuitable by the investigator. Past or current non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids. Active autoimmune diseases necessitating systemic therapy within the preceding twelve months or historically severe autoimmune syndromes (exceptions noted for vitiligo and remote mild asthma/allergy). Significant morphological abnormalities on baseline peripheral blood smear suggestive of myeloproliferative neoplasm, myelodysplastic syndrome, or acute leukemias. Personal history of myeloid malignancies, myelodysplastic syndrome, lymphoblastic lymphoma, or acute lymphoblastic/leukemia. Previous solid organ transplantation. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Prior administration of any anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑CTLA‑4, or other T‑cell costimulatory/co‑inhibitory pathway modulators. Receipt of live or attenuated vaccinations within thirty days before first study drug dosing. Documented hypersensitivity to tazemetostat, niravimod, ipilimumab, or structurally related substances. Inability to comply with scheduled safety assessments and monitoring procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 6 months to 21 years. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Rhabdoid tumor of the kidney. Histology allowed: INI1‑negative or SMARCA4‑deficient. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable disease (relapsed/refractory), evaluatable disease (pre‑recurrence) or no evidence of disease (remission). Prior treatment required: Completion of planned upfront therapy for the disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, anti‑CTLA‑4, OX40 or CD137 targeted agents; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant; prior EZH2 inhibitor therapy (except in relapsed/refractory cohort). Biomarkers required: Loss of INI1 protein expression by immunohistochemistry OR loss of SMARCA4 protein expression by immunohistochemistry (molecular confirmation accepted). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

general_exclusion_criteria:
* Presence of a locoregional solid tumor deemed resectable or curable with definitive surgery/radiation/chemo.  
* History of another malignant neoplasm (with protocol-specified exceptions).  
* History of lymphohistiocytic or lymphoid hyperplasia; haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.  
* Failure to return to baseline health or grade ≤ 1 toxicity from preceding anticancer therapy.  
* Clinically significant cardiovascular events occurring within six months prior to enrollment.  
* Known active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.  
* Current active interstitial lung disease requiring therapeutic intervention.  
* Past or current uveitis, retinitis, or other meaningful retinal pathology.  
* Active or chronic corneal disease necessitating ongoing ophthalmologic treatment.  
* Recent surgical wounds: major surgery within one month or minor procedure within ten days before dosing.  
* Confirmed Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, active Hepatitis B, or active Hepatitis C infection.  
* Pre-existing hepatic impairment classified as Child‑Pugh B or C, or any other substantial liver disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Female and Male. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Node-positive, early stage (no distant metastasis), high risk of recurrence. Prior treatment required: Minimum 24 months and maximum 60 months of aromatase inhibitor or tamoxifen endocrine therapy (with or without concomitant CDK4/6 inhibitor). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive (≥10% by immunohistochemistry) and human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (Immunohistochemistry 0 or 1, or Immunohistochemistry 2 with in situ hybridisation negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Inflammatory breast cancer.
History of any prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer.
Any malignant neoplasm diagnosed within three years preceding randomization, excluding adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer, superficial bladder cancer, or cervical intraepithelial neoplasia/carcinoma in situ.
More than six consecutive months’ interruption of prior standard-of-care adjuvant endocrine therapy or cessation of adjuvant endocrine therapy greater than six months before randomization.
Active untreated central nervous system metastases.
Uncontrolled interstitial lung disease or recent pneumonitis.
Significant cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class III-IV).
Severe hepatic impairment (e.g., Child-Pugh C) or bilirubin elevation exceeding institutional limits.
Renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance less than institution-specified threshold, e.g., <30 mL/min).
Human immunodeficiency virus infection or active hepatitis B/C infection.
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group grade 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: greater than 1 year and younger than 25 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: B‑cell acute lymphoblastic leukaemia. Histology allowed: B‑cell lineage acute lymphoblastic leukaemia (high‑risk) confirmed by WHO 2016 criteria with ≥25 % blasts in bone marrow or equivalent. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease; meets age‑specified white blood cell count criteria (≤ 50,000/µL for ages 1–9.99 years unless testicular or CNS involvement or steroid pretreatment; unrestricted for ages 10–24.99 years). Prior treatment required: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of transformed/aggressive lymphoma,
Unresolved serious adverse event from prior anticancer therapy (grade > 1 residual toxicity),
Active uncontrolled infection requiring inpatient care or IV antibiotics,
Severe cardiac disease (NYHA class III–IV heart failure, left ventricular ejection fraction < 45%, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, significant arrhythmia),
Significant pulmonary compromise needing supplemental oxygen at rest,
Chronic immunosuppressive medication usage (incl. systemic glucocorticoids ≥20 mg prednisolone equivalent daily) for non‑study indications,
Central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia or leptomeningeal disease,
Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to comply with mandated contraception,
Substance abuse, untreated major psychiatric illness, or socially unstable circumstances limiting protocol adherence,
Known hypersensitivity to epcoritamab or structurally related substances,
Recent administration of live or live‑attenuated vaccination (within 4 weeks).

Men with hormone‑dependent cancers (breast, prostate, adenocarcinoma of unknown origin).

Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.

Untreated unipolar depressive illness (depression treated with medication or counselling permitted).  
Bipolar affective disorder or schizophrenia.

Men with hormone‑dependent cancers (breast, prostate, adenocarcinoma of unknown origin).  
Men with central nervous system (brain) cancer.  
Baseline hematocrit >48%.  
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.  
Severe untreated obstructive sleep apnoea.  
Recent myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, cardiac revascularisation procedure, or cerebrovascular accident/stroke occurring within 90 days before enrollment.  
Residual neurological deficit from prior stroke resulting in Mini‑Mental State Examination score <24.  
Serum creatinine concentration >2.5 mg/dL.  
Alanine aminotransferase >3 times the institutional upper limit of normal.  
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus indicated by haemoglobin A1c >8.5%.  
Body mass index >45 kg/m².  
Untreated unipolar depressive illness (depression treated with medication or counselling permitted).  
Bipolar affective disorder or schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reaction to humanized or murine monoclonal antibodies, hypersensitivity to murine products, or to dexamethasone/systemic corticosteroids. Prior organ transplantation. Diagnosis of indolent lymphoma or current/following histories of follicular lymphoma grade 3B, grey‑zone lymphoma, primary mediastinal (thymic) large b‑cell lymphoma, burkitt lymphoma, cns lymphoma (primary or secondary), primary effusion dlbcl, primary cutaneous dlbcl. Presence of active hepatitis b or c infection with detectable viral loads; unresolved hbv replication despite suppression. Positive hiv or htlv‑1 serology. Significant cardiovascular disease (nyha class III/IV, class C/D cardiac disease, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable arrhythmias, unstable angina). Severe respiratory disease. Uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system disease, including stroke or intracranial haemorrhage within three months, or ongoing seizure disorders. Peripheral neuropathy grade 2 or higher or demyelinating charcot‑marty toe disease. Recent major surgery within four weeks preceding enrolment. Active autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive therapy (except controlled hypothyroidism on levothyroxine or type 1 diabetes on basal insulin). Current pregnancy or breastfeeding, or inability to comply with mandated contraception/sperm donation rules. Ongoing acute infection requiring intravenous antibiotics or hospitalisation, or reactivated latent infection. Clinically significant liver disease (active viral hepatitis, cirrhosis, alcoholic liver disease). Renal insufficiency failing laboratory thresholds. Poor performance status beyond ecog 2. Lack of available tumour tissue for biomarker evaluation. Investigator judgement deeming participation unsafe or unlikely to yield interpretable data.

Histology allowed: CD20‑positive diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma (excludes follicular lymphoma grade 3B, transformed indolent B‑cell lymphomas, mediastinal gray‑zone lymphoma, primary mediastinal (thymic) large B‑cell lymphoma, Burkitt lymphoma, primary large B‑cell lymphoma of immune‑privileged sites—including primary central nervous system, vitreoretinal, and testicular involvement—as well as primary effusion diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma, primary cutaneous diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma, leg type, and primary or secondary central nervous system lymphoma). general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe allergic or anaphylactic reaction to humanised or murine monoclonal antibodies or murine products; current grade > 1 peripheral neuropathy or demyelinating Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease; significant or extensive cardiovascular disease; clinically significant hepatic impairment; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy; ongoing serious infection or fever within seven days; recent major surgical procedure within four weeks; evidence of active or latent tuberculosis; positive testing for chronic hepatitis B, hepatitis C, or HTLV‑1 infection; known or presumed chronic active Epstein‑Barr virus infection; suspicion of haemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis; prior progression‑free interval less than stipulated for washouts ignored here; history of progressive multifocal leucoencephalopathy; administration of live attenuated vaccination within four weeks preceding enrolment or anticipated need for such vaccine during the study.

Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure or reduced left ventricular function. Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance < 60 ml/min/1.73 m²) or dialysis dependence. Clinically meaningful hepatic insufficiency (bilirubin > 3× ULN or transaminases > 5× ULN). Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastasis. Current use of additional antitumor therapies (other chemotherapy, targeted agents, hormonal therapy, radiation) apart from mandated wash‑out exemptions. Continuous immune suppression for graft‑versus‑host disease prevention or organ transplantation. Recent grade ≥ 3 infectious complications, unresolved febrile neutropenia, or active invasive fungal, bacterial, viral, or parasitic infection requiring intravenous therapy. Documented hypersensitivity to any study medication components. Presence of inherited DNA repair disorders (e.g., Fanconi anaemia, Bloom syndrome) or other genetic syndromes compromising safe participation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma (all histologic subtypes). Cancer burden allowed: Primary tumor pathological stage pT1‑3 (minimum T1c if node‑negative); ipsilateral axillary nodes pN0 (including isolated tumor cells regarded as N0) or pN1 mi/a/b/c (micrometastasis counts as N1); no distant metastatic disease; stages corresponding to AJCC 8th edition IA‑IIIA. Prior treatment required: Completion of definitive surgery of the index breast lesion (total/mastectomy, skin‑sparring/nipple‑saving mastectomy, or lumpectomy) with microscopically negative margins (except allowance for LCIS positivity or unavoidable close/posterior margin); axillary staging performed by sentinel node biopsy, targeted axillary dissection, or axillary lymph node dissection; plan for postoperative radiation therapy documented before entry. Prior treatment excluded: Cytotoxic chemotherapy, biologic therapy, or radiation therapy administered for the current breast cancer; endocrine therapy lasting more than six weeks for the current cancer; concomitant use of hormonally based contraceptives at baseline. Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor positive (≥1 % nuclei) **and**/or progesterone‑receptor positive (≥1 % nuclei); human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative by ASCO/CAP criteria; Oncotype DX recurrence score 21‑25; confirmation of premenopausal status by menstrual history/female ≤ 50 yr with cycles within past year or biochemical verification (follicle stimulating hormone and estradiol in premenopausal range). Biomarkers excluded: MammaPrint high‑risk (MP2) result without qualifying Oncotype DX score; absence of ER/PR positivity; HER2 amplification.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Definitive clinical or radiographic evidence of metastatic disease.
Pathologic T4 tumours, including inflammatory breast cancer.
History of prior ipsilateral or contralateral invasive breast cancer (excluding prior DCIS or LCIS).
Life expectancy shorter than ten years owing to comorbid illness.
Absolute neutrophil count <1200/mm³, platelet count <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <10 g/dL.
Total bilirubin >ULN (or >1.5×ULN with benign hyperbilirubinemia), AST/ALT >3×ULN.
Estimated glomerular filtration rate <30 ml/min/1.73 m².
Cardiac disease placing the patient worse than NYHA Class 2B or equivalent functional limitation.
Non‑epithelial breast malignancies (sarcoma, lymphoma, etc.).
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at the time of registration; inadequate birth control compliance.
Active chronic hepatitis B infection (unless suppressed with undetectable viral load) or unresolved hepatitis C infection (unless cured or virologically suppressed).
