clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non‑squamous. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s) per RECIST v1.1. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic anticancer therapy for advanced/metastatic disease; prior curative‑intent surgery±adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy permissible if completed ≥6 months before diagnosis of advanced/metastatic disease and without disease progression within 6 months thereafter. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic therapy for advanced/metastatic non‑small cell lung cancer; any prior receipt of a topoisomerase I‑containing antibody‑drug conjugate; any prior TROP2‑targeted therapy; any prior anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2 antibody; any prior other immune‑checkpoint modulator (e.g., CTLA‑4, OX40, CD137) agents. Biomarkers required: PD‑L1 tumour proportion score <50% by centralized 22C3 IHC; confirmed absence of actionable genomic alterations (e.g., EGFR sensitising mutation, ALK, ROS1, RET, NTRK, MET exon 14 skipping, BRAF V600E); submission of tumor tissue for assessment of TROP2 protein expression. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic spinal cord compression; clinically active untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; unresolved cerebral edema or neurologic symptoms due to brain metastasis; requirement for corticosteroids greater than physiologic doses within 7 days preceding enrollment; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease—including QTcF >470 ms, myocardial infarction ≤6 months, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled angina ≤6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%, New York Heart Association class II–IV heart failure not stabilized to class I with LVEF ≥50%; uncontrolled arterial hypertension (>180/110 mm Hg). Severe pulmonary impairment such as recent (<3 months) pulmonary embolism, severe asthma, severe COPD, restrictive lung disease, extensive pleural effusion, interstitial lung disease, or prior total pneumonectomy. Recent live vaccination within 30 days (excluding standard injectable flu vaccine); investigational COVID‑19 vaccines; current pregnancy or breastfeeding; inability to undergo mandatory baseline MRI/contrast-enhanced CT of the brain; concurrent malignancy requiring active treatment; ECOG/Karnofsky performance status indicating inadequate functional capacity (generally PS ≥2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: any non‑small cell lung cancer histology (adenocarcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma, large‑cell carcinoma, etc.) excluding mixed small cell/non‑small cell histology. Cancer burden allowed: clinically staged IIIA or IIIB (tumor size T1‑to‑T3, N2 nodal disease) that is deemed potentially resectable with intention of R0 resection; N2 nodes must be discrete and ≤3 cm in maximal dimension; no evidence of N3 nodal disease or distant metastasis; T4 tumors excluded. Prior treatment required: none. Prior treatment excluded: any prior systemic anticancer therapy for non‑small cell lung cancer, any prior thoracic radiation, any prior surgical resection of the target lesion. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of Grade ≥2 peripheral neuropathy; active or past autoimmune disease requiring systemic steroids/immunosuppression (excluding vitiligo, resolved childhood asthma/atopy, isolated hypothyroidism, alopecia, chronic skin conditions without systemic therapy, well‑controlled celiac disease, or autoimmune disease inactive for >5 years after specialist approval); current systemic corticosteroid use exceeding 10 mg/day prednisolone equivalent within 14 days of dosing; prior malignant diagnoses other than certain early‐stage skin, bladder, gastric, breast, colorectal, or cervical lesions unless in complete remission ≥2 years and off therapy; solid organ transplantation; pregnancy or lactation; uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as active infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension, unstable angina, significant cardiac arrhythmias, interstitial lung disease, severe GI disorder causing diarrhoea, or psychiatric/socioeconomic factors limiting adherence; baseline QTcF ≥470 ms.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast cancer (including inflammatory breast cancer). Cancer burden allowed: unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease; treated and radiographically stable brain metastasis permissible. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: current receipt of any other anticancer therapy; disease that has relapsed or progressed less than 6 months after the most recent taxane‐based therapy; NA for line limitations (no limit on number of prior chemotherapy lines for metastatic disease). Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor ≤10% AND progesterone receptor ≤10% AND human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (immunohistochemistry score 0‑1+ or fluorescence in situ hybridisation negative). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe or unresolved pneumonia/pneumonitis.
Unstable or symptomatic congestive heart failure (New York Heart Association class II or greater).
Recent (<6 weeks) major surgery.
Active uncontrolled infection, including COVID‑19, HIV with detectable viremia, hepatitis B or C with active replication.
Significant pulmonary disease causing dyspnea at rest or minimal exertion.
Grade 2 or higher peripheral neuropathy.
Baseline corrected QT interval (Fridericia) >480 ms or use of drugs that markedly prolong QT/QTC.
Known hypersensitivity to paclitaxel.
Inability to ingest/swallow oral medication.
Clinically significant untreated or progressive central nervous system involvement (except stable treated brain metastases meeting stability criteria).
Strong inhibitors or inducer agents of cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme activity.

Prior treatment excluded: current receipt of any other anticancer therapy; disease that has relapsed or progressed less than 6 months after the most recent taxane‑based therapy; more than three lines of chemotherapy for metastatic disease. Biomarkers required: estrogen receptor ≤10% AND progesterone receptor ≤10% AND human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (immunohistochemistry score 0‑1+ or fluorescence in situ hybrdisation negative). clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: any cancer type (solid tumors or hematologic malignancies). Histology allowed: any. Cancer burden allowed: presence of at least one diagnostic/chronic graft‑versus‑host disease manifestation (involvement of any organ system); no evidence of active malignancy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic immune‑suppressive therapy for chronic graft‑versus‑host disease (topical or localized therapies acceptable); prednisone or prednisone‑equivalent dose ≥0.25 mg/kg/day at screening; any prior exposure to belumosudil; receipt of another investigational agent concurrently. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of non‑compliance interfering with study participation.
Uncontrolled psychiatric illness.
Female participant who is pregnant or breastfeeding.
Known allergy or hypersensitivity to belumosudil or related ROCK2 inhibitors.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Head and neck cancer. Histology allowed: Squamous or nonsquamous carcinoma arising from oral cavity, salivary gland, oropharynx, hypopharynx, or larynx (nasopharyngeal primaries excluded). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally recurrent or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Documented disease progression after receiving a platinum‑based systemic therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression (immunohistochemistry intensity 1+, 2+, or 3+) or HER2 activating mutation detected in tumor tissue. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Prior treatment with an monomethyl auristatin E‑containing agent.
Known hypersensitivity to any ingredient of disitamab vedotin formulation.
History of another invasive malignant neoplasm within two years before enrollment or existing residual disease from a prior cancer.
Active untreated central nervous system involvement or leptomeningeal carcinomatous meningitis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: Any pathological subtype of non‑small cell lung cancer. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Progressive disease after platinum‑based therapy (either concurrent with locally advanced/metastatic setting or within six months after completed/adjuvant/neoadjuvant platinum‑containing chemoradiation) AND prior administration of an anti‑programmed death‑ligand 1/programmed death‑1 immune checkpoint inhibitor (unless clinically contraindicated); if a pathogenic actionable genomic alteration is identified, matching targeted therapy must have been delivered whenever available. Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of more than two prior lines of cytotoxic chemotherapy for advanced disease. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression (IHC 1+, 2+, or 3+) or HER2 activating mutation in tumor tissue. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal carcinoma. Histology allowed: Epithelial carcinoma. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression (IHC 1+, 2+, or 3+) or HER2 activating mutation detectable in tumour tissue. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: glioma. Histology allowed: WHO grade 2 glioma or WHO grade 3 glioma. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or progressively growing disease with at least one measurable lesion. Prior treatment required: up to two prior therapies administered for disease recurrence or progression. Prior treatment excluded: any prior therapy directed toward IDH1 mutation (IDH1‑targeted therapy). Biomarkers required: IDH1 R132H mutation or IDH1 R132C mutation verified by immunohistochemistry or molecular genetic testing. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity to safusidenib or chemically similar compounds.
Brainstem or spinal cord involvement as primary site, multicentric spread, or extensive infiltration.
Diffuse leptomeningeal disease evidenced by magnetic resonance imaging.
Uncontrolled seizures or significant neurocognitive impairment preventing compliance with study procedures.
Serious cardiovascular disease occurring within 12 months preceding randomization.
Requirement for escalating doses of corticosteroids; subjects must be on a stable or tapering regimen.
Concurrent malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment or less than three years disease‑free interval (except cured basal/squamous non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ).
Any medical condition judged by investigator to substantially increase risk or impede adherence to protocol obligations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: astrocytoma. Histology allowed: WHO grade 3 astrocytoma with high‑risk features OR WHO grade 4 astrocytoma, both harboring an IDH1 mutation. Cancer burden allowed: no radiographically evident disease recurrence or progression after completion of definitive therapy. Prior treatment required: completed external beam radiation therapy delivering at least 90% of planned dose (with or without concurrent temozolomide) and finished six to twelve cycles of adjuvant temozolomide; prior surgery permissible; prior tumor‑treating fields allowable only if discontinued before randomization. Prior treatment excluded: any anticancer therapy other than surgery, radiation, concurrent/adjuvant temozolomide, or tumor‑treating fields; anti‑angiogenic agents (e.g., bevacizumab); agents targeting IDH1 or IDH2; investigational agents for glioma; continuation of tumor‑treating fields at randomization. Biomarkers required: IDH1 mutation (R132H, R132C, R132G, R132S, or R132L) established by IHC, PCR, or NGS; documentation of CDKN2A/B status; and at least one of the following—a proven absence of 1p/19q co‑deletion, detection of an ATRX loss‑of‑function mutation, or loss of normal ATRX expression—all obtained through a CLIA‑certified / CAP‑ accredited laboratory assay. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity to safusidenib or chemically similar compounds.

Concurrent malignant neoplasm requiring active treatment or less than three years disease‑free interval (except cured basal/squamous non‑melanoma skin cancer or carcinoma in situ).
Any medical condition judged by investigator to substantially increase risk or impede adherence to protocol obligations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >= 65 years. Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Stages I through III, non‑metastatic (absence of distant metastasis). Prior treatment required: Planned to receive standard‑dose neoadjuvant or adjuvant docetaxel plus cyclophosphamide (TC) chemotherapy (immunotherapy permitted concurrently at investigator’s discretion). Prior treatment excluded: Any chemotherapy previously administered for the current breast cancer **and** prior receipt of a docetaxel plus cyclophosphamide regimen for a former breast cancer. Biomarkers required: HER2‑negative (tested according to 2018 ASCO‐CAP guidelines) **and** documented estrogen receptor and/or progesterone receptor status (any result accepted). Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe pulmonary toxicities including pneumonitis, clinically unstable cardiac conditions (e.g., congestive heart failure NYHA class III/IV), uncontrolled hypertension, significant arrhythmias, myocardial infarction within 6 months, serious hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh B or C), severe renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min), untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases, ongoing infections requiring intravenous antimicrobial therapy, known HIV infection or chronic viral hepatitis B/C with detectable viremia, autoimmune diseases requiring systemic steroids/immunosuppression, pregnant or breastfeeding individuals, Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Invasive breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive carcinoma (ductal, lobular or other invasive histologies). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic anticancer therapy for breast cancer. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor negative **and** progesterone receptor negative **and** human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 negative (assessed according to ASCO/CAP guidelines). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of inflammatory breast cancer or locally recurrent breast cancer.
Pregnancy or lactation.
Uncontrolled concurrent serious medical conditions including active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction (<6 months), clinically significant cardiac repolarisation abnormalities, systolic blood pressure >160 mmHg or <90 mmHg, resting heart rate <50 bpm, unrecoverable QTc (>470 ms) or unable to obtain readable ECG.
Known HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy.
Active hepatitis B virus infection or documented chronic hepatitis C infection, or history thereof.
Any concomitant systemic therapy for another malignant disease within three years (time‑limited washout omitted from space definition).
Severe hepatic impairment (alanine transaminase/asparate transaminase >2.5× upper normal limit, total bilirubin >1.5×ULN except compensated Gilbert’s syndrome) or severe renal insufficiency (creatinine >1.5 mg/dL or CrCl <50 mL/min).

Central nervous system disorder, including stroke, transient ischemic attack, or posterior reversible encephalopathy syndrome within twelve months prior to enrolment.
Active hepatitis B or C infection.
Human immunodeficiency virus positivity unless adequately treated with undetectable viral load and CD4 > 200 cells/µL.
Medical conditions or residual toxicities from prior therapies expected to interfere with safety or efficacy assessments.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease occurring within twelve months before enrollment.
Requirement for continuous systemic immunosuppressive or disease-modifying therapy within the past two years.

Renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min) or dialysis dependence.
Active central nervous system involvement or untreated/uncontrolled brain metastases.
Concurrent serious infection, immunodeficiency, or uncontrolled HIV/Hepatitis B/C outside stipulated control parameters.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2.
Pregnancy, lactation, or unwillingness to use effective contraception (not applicable to male cohort but retained as protocol blanket).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male or Female. Cancer type allowed: Hepatocellular carcinoma. Histology allowed: Conventional hepatocellular carcinoma (excluding fibrolamellar, sarcomatoid, or biphenotypic variants). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable disease not amenable to surgical resection, transplant, or radiofrequency ablation; up to 5 intra‑hepatic lesions with total maximum dimension <20 cm and no single lesion >15 cm; at least one measurable lesion (≥10 mm on CT/MRI or ≥20 mm on chest X‑ray); permits small‑volume extrahepatic disease whose summed longest dimensions <2 cm; Barcelona Clinic Liver Cancer stage Intermediate (B) or Advanced (C); Child‑Pugh class A; no significant portal hypertension. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic anticancer therapy; any prior selective internal radiotherapy (SIRT) or hepatic arterial Yttrium microsphere therapy; any prior liver‑directed external beam radiation that would deliver excessive dose to normal liver tissue; any prior liver irradiation overlapping planned radiotherapy fields; prior allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell or solid organ transplantation. Biomarkers required: Documented hepatitis B virus serology; documented hepatitis C virus serology; HIV‑negative status. Biomarkers excluded: Concurrent active hepatitis B and hepatitis C infection.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of autoimmune disease (except isolated hypothyroidism, controlled type 1 diabetes, mild eczema/psoriasis/lichen/simplex/chronic dermatitis meeting strict criteria).
Active or past pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, or unexplained pulmonary infiltrates.
Significant cardiovascular disease (NYHA class II or higher, recent MI, stroke, unstable angina, uncontrolled arrhythmias).
Uncontrolled hypertension (systolic ≥150 mmHg or diastolic >100 mmHg despite therapy).
Severe infection within four weeks, including hospitalized bacterial/fungal/viral infections.
Active tuberculosis.
Clinically significant bleeding disorders or coagulation abnormalities.
Recent major surgery or invasive procedures violating stipulated intervals.
Use of full‑dose anticoagulants or antiplatelet agents beyond approved low‑risk regimens.
Untreated or inadequately treated gastroesophageal varices at high risk of bleeding.
Moderate or severe ascites, refractory hepatic encephalopathy, or uncontrolled tumor‑related pain.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraception in women of child‑bearing potential; men unwilling to comply with condom use or abstinence recommendations.
Live attenuated vaccinations within four weeks before study start or anticipated thereafter.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥12 months and <30 years. Sex allowed: both sexes. Cancer type allowed: neuroblastoma. Histology allowed: neuroblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: high‑risk neuroblastoma per Children’s Oncology Group classification **and** persistent/refractory or relapsed disease for which standard curative measures are no longer effective **and** evaluable or measurable disease per the Revised International Neuroblastoma Response Criteria. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior CAR T‑cell therapy *or* other gene‑modified immune‑effector cell therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Pregnancy or lactation. Active uncontrolled infections. Concurrent receipt of other investigational agents. Presence of an additional malignancy other than non‑melanomatous skin cancer or carcinoma in situ, unless inactive/stable without recent treatment. Uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis. Significant baseline central nervous system disorders (e.g., stroke, hemorrhage, demyelinating disease, autoimmune encephalopathy). Severe hypersensitivity reactions to study drug components. Positive serology for HIV, HBV, or HCV. Ongoing systemic corticosteroid therapy exceeding physiologic doses (inhaled steroids and peri‑transfusion prophylaxis permitted). Primary immunodeficiency or systemic autoimmune disease requiring immunosuppressive or disease‑modifying drugs within the past 2 years. Uncontrolled comorbid illnesses. Performance status lower than required (Lansky/Karnofsky <50%). Inability to meet study compliance requirements.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: all sexes. Cancer type allowed: Waldenström macroglobulinemia. Histology allowed: lymphoplasmacytic lymphoma with IgM secretion. Cancer burden allowed: bone marrow infiltration by small lymphocytes with plasmacytoid differentiation together with an IgM monoclonal paraprotein and clinically evident predominantly sensory demyelinating peripheral neuropathy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy, Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors, or other Waldénström macroglobulinaemia‐specific therapies (exempting corticosteroids, intravenous immune globulin, or anti‑CD20 antibodies administered >90 days before first study dose). Biomarkers required: detection of an IgM monoclonal paraprotein by serum immunofixation; bone‑marrow specimen demonstrating surface IgM⁺, CD5±, CD10⁻, CD19⁺, CD20⁺, CD22⁺, CD23⁻, CD25⁺, CD27⁺, FMC7⁺, CD103⁻, CD138⁻ immunophenotype (assessed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: all sexes. Cancer type allowed: IgM monoclonal gammopathy of undetermined significance. Histology allowed: IgM MGUS (absence of overt bone‑marrow infiltration). Cancer burden allowed: presence of an IgM monoclonal paraprotein of any concentration accompanied by a predominantly sensory demyelinating peripheral neuropathy fulfilling modified Rankin scale ≥1 with progression or ≥2 overall, without evidence of underlying hematologic malignancy. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: systemic chemotherapy, Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitors, or other Waldénström macroglobulinaemia‑targeted therapies (exceptions identical to Space 1). Biomarkers required: detection of an IgM monoclonal paraprotein by serum immunofixation; lack of bone‑marrow infiltration by clonal lymphoplasmacytic cells (bone‑marrow assessment confirming absent infiltration). Biomarkers excluded: NA.

Age range allowed: <50 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: ≤4 pulmonary nodules per lung, each ≥3 mm and ≤3 cm, radiologically resectable, no extrapulmonary metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: receipt of standard systemic chemotherapy containing cisplatin together with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen administered within 60 days of enrollment (ongoing or recently completed); definitive surgical control of the primary tumor performed contemporaneously with thoracic surgery or already achieved. Prior treatment excluded: therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastasis prior to enrollment. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Unresectable primary tumor; pulmonary metastatic lesions necessitating anatomical resections such as lobectomy or pneumonectomy or classified as central lesions involving/proximal to segmental bronchi; chest wall or mediastinal–based metastatic lesions or clinically significant pleural effusion; documented disease progression at the primary or pulmonary metastatic sites while on initial therapy; existence of extrapulmonary metastatic disease; prior therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastasis; inability or refusal to provide signed informed consent; failure to meet applicable Institutional Review Board, Food and Drug Administration, and National Cancer Institute regulations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: <50 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: osteosarcoma. Histology allowed: osteosarcoma. Cancer burden allowed: ≤4 pulmonary nodules per lung, each ≥3 mm and ≤3 cm, radiologically resectable, no extrapulmonary metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: completion of standard systemic chemotherapy containing cisplatin together with doxorubicin **or** an ifosfamide‑based regimen for the original primary tumor prior to enrollment (at time of first recurrence). general_exclusion_criteria: Unresectable primary tumor; pulmonary metastatic lesions necessitating anatomical resections such as lobectomy or pneumonectomy or classified as central lesions involving/proximal to segmental bronchi; chest wall or mediastinal–based metastatic lesions or clinically significant pleural effusion; documented disease progression at the primary or pulmonary metastatic sites while on initial therapy; existence of extrapulmonary metastatic disease; prior therapeutic pulmonary surgery for lung metastasis; inability or refusal to provide signed informed consent; failure to meet applicable Institutional Review Board, Food and Drug Administration, and National Cancer Institute regulations.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Follicular lymphoma. Histology allowed: Grade 1‑3A, CD20 positive. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior systemic therapy for follicular lymphoma (including chemotherapy, targeted agents, immunotherapeutic agents) . general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active hepatitis B or C infection, or chronic unresolved hepatitis B/C infection.
Known HIV infection.
Active or recent (<2 weeks) bacterial, viral, fungal, mycobacterial, parasitic, or other infection (excluding superficial nail fungus).
Significant cardiovascular disease (recent MI, unstable angina, NYHA class III‑IV heart failure, EF<45%, uncontrolled arrhythmias, prolonged QTc >470 ms, peripheral arterial disease needing reintervention).
Severe pulmonary disorders including active pneumonitis.
Renal insufficiency (CrCl <45 mL/min) or hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >1.5 ×ULN, ALT/AST >2.5 ×ULN absent liver involvement).
Uncontrolled central nervous system involvement or active CNS lymphoma.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intention to conceive during study.
Recent major surgery or trauma within 4 weeks.
Live attenuated vaccination within recommended interval.
Previous allogenic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.
History of hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis.
Autoimmune diseases deemed moderate-to-severe or uncontrolled (myocarditis, pneumonitis, Myasthenia Gravis, SLE, RA, IBD, vasculitides, MS, etc.) unless approved by sponsor.
Severe hypersensitivity to anti‑CD20 antibodies or components of epcoritamab.
Neuropathy greater than grade 2.
Active COVID‑19 infection or inability to confirm virologic clearance.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma. Prior treatment required: Completion of neoadjuvant chemotherapy followed by definitive breast surgery (lumpectomy or mastectomy) with clear margins and appropriate postoperative radiation when indicated; detection of circulating tumour DNA in plasma after completion of all locoregional therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior sacituzumab govitecan, irinotecan, or any topoisomerase I–containing antibody‑drug conjugate administered for early‑stage disease; receipt of adjuvant (post‑surgical) chemotherapy. Biomarkers required: HER2 negative (ISH ratio < 2.0 or average HER2 copy number < 4 signals per cell, or IHC score 0 or 1+) and Estrogen Receptor negative (<10% cells stained) and Progesterone Receptor negative (<10% cells stained) and Presence of any tumor‑specific mutation in circulating tumour DNA. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia, drug‑induced pneumonitis, or active pneumonitis on imaging.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association class III/IV heart failure, myocardial infarction within six months, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias).
Serious intercurrent illnesses including active infection requiring systemic therapy, active tuberculosis, cirrhosis or other severe liver disease, uncontrolled type I or type II diabetes, uncontrolled hypertension, dyslipidaemia, hypertriglyceridaemia, or hypercalcaemia.
Clinically significant gastrointestinal disorders that markedly affect drug absorption (severe ulcerative colitis, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhoea grade ≥ 2, malabsorption syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resection).
Congenital long QT syndrome or baseline corrected QT interval >480 milliseconds.
Known positive tests for Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Hepatitis B surface antigen, or Hepatitis C viral RNA.
Current pregnancy or lactation.
History of prior invasive breast cancer in either breast.
Previous malignancies other than breast cancer unless disease‑free for at least five years (exception made for adequately treated, regionally confined thyroid cancer).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: male and female. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: any breast cancer histology. general_exclusion_criteria: Active participation in another therapeutic clinical trial. Presence of active, uncontrolled or symptomatic central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Recent (within 2 weeks) palliative radiotherapy to a limited field or extensive radiotherapy involving >30 % of bone marrow. Significant recent surgery or trauma (within 14 days). Cardiac conditions including NYHA class II–IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, left ventricular ejection fraction <55 %, poorly controlled hypertension (SBP ≥180 mmHg or DBP ≥100 mmHg), symptomatic arrhythmias requiring intervention, or congenital/acquired Long QT syndrome/QTc prolongation (>470 ms females, >450 ms males). Severe pulmonary comorbidity such as recent pulmonary embolism, moderate/severe COPD, interstitial lung disease/history of pneumonitis, or prior pneumonectomy. Pregnancy, lactation, or inability/unwillingness to use highly effective contraception. Active hepatitis B or C infection, or HIV infection. Ongoing uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection. Live or attenuated vaccination within 30 days preceding study treatment. Substance misuse or unmanaged psychiatric illness compromising adherence. Any other uncontrolled medically serious condition deemed unsuitable by the investigators.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Subjects with tumour lesion(s) located where GCC19CART therapy may cause perforation of an organ or structure (e.g., gastrointestinal tract, urinary bladder, major vessels).
Active infectious diseases or comorbid conditions that would interfere with safety or data integrity.

Pregnancy or lactation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with visceral metastases.
Active herpetic infections or prior complications of HSV‑1 infection (e.g., herpetic keratitis or encephalitis).
History of organ graft rejection within 12 months.
Systemic infection requiring IV antibiotics or antivirals, or other serious infection within 60 days before dosing.
Requirement for intermittent or chronic systemic antiretroviral agents with anti‑herpes activity (unless for graft preservation).
Use of CTLA‑4–Ig medications.
Significant autoimmune disease treated with systemic immunosuppression beyond routine graft prophylaxis within past 5 years.
Active infection with hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus.
Any transplant‑related viral infection (BK virus, Epstein-Barr virus, Cytomegalovirus) within 3 months; detectable HBV or HCV viral loads within 3 months.
Condition necessitating escalation of baseline immunosuppressive regimen within 60 days of study treatment.
Known active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe heart disease including symptomatic congestive heart failure grade ≥2, NYHA class ≥2, myocardium infarction, or unstable angina. Prolonged corrected QT interval greater than 470 ms, complete left bundle branch block, or grade 3 AV block. Active autoimmune or inflammatory disorders requiring systemic therapy (excluding well‑controlled hypothyroidism, type I diabetes, mild skin disease). Additional malignancies diagnosed within five years except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin cancer. Poorly controlled hypertension (SBP >150 mmHg or DBP >100 mmHg despite two agents). Grade 3 or higher preexisting lung disease, interstitial lung disease, or past pneumonitis. Recent unresolved thrombotic events (deep venous thrombosis, arterial thrombosis, pulmonary embolism) within six months. Untreated or active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; recently treated brain metastases must be stable ≥4 weeks and off steroids >20 mg prednisolone daily. Allergy to recombinant humanized or mouse‑chimeric antibodies or any component of the study drug. Previous hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Cumulative anthracycline exposure >360 mg/m². Grade 2 or worse hypokalaemia. Positive HIV antibody, active hepatitis B or C infection, or active tuberculosis. Ongoing acute infections needing systemic therapy. Pregnant or lactating individuals. Any condition judged by investigators to render participation unsafe.

Hematocrit greater than 48%.
Serum creatinine greater than 2.5 mg/dL.
Prostate‑specific antigen greater than 4 ng/mL or palpable nodules/indurations on digital rectal examination.
Severe untreated sleep apnoea.
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure.
Myocardial infarction, acute coronary syndrome, cardiac revascularisation, stroke or arterial / venous thromboembolism occurring within six months.
Known inherited thrombophilia (e.g., factor V Leiden).
Previous cerebrovascular accident leaving persistent neurological deficits.
Cognitive impairment preventing consent (Mini–Mental State Examination score <24).
Poorly controlled diabetes mellitus (hemoglobin A1c >10%).  
Body mass index exceeding 40 kg/m².  
Bipolar disorder or schizophrenia.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis  
Uncontrolled congestive heart failure or symptomatic cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, significant arrhythmia)  
Renal impairment exceeding protocol limits (creatinine >1.5× ULN or creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min)  
Hepatic dysfunction exceeding protocol limits (bilirubin >1.5× ULN; transaminases >2.5–5× ULN)  
Active central nervous system metastasis requiring immediate radiation or surgical intervention (symptomatic CNS lesions)  
Known HIV‑positive serostatus  
Active chronic hepatitis B or C infection  
Pregnancy or lactation (women of child‑bearing potential must have negative pregnancy test and use contraception)  
Use of enzyme‑inducing anti‑seizure drugs that cannot be switched away before starting study drug  
Severe hypersensitivity reaction to abemaciosl or structurally related compounds  
Inability to comply with study procedures owing to psychiatric or social conditions.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Autologous or allogeneic stem cell transplant within 6 months prior to Cycle 1 Day 1.
Receiving immunosuppressants (e.g., cyclosporin) or systemic steroids (except steroid used solely for cortisol replacement in confirmed adrenal insufficiency).
Active fungal disease or uncontrolled infection of any kind; patients on antibiotics, antivirals, or antifungals must be afebrile and hemodynamically stable for longer than 72 hours prior to treatment.
White blood cell count greater than 25 × 10⁹/L measured within 7 days prior to the first dose of eganelisib (use of hydroxyurea to lower count is permitted).
Presence of a clinically significant non‑hematologic toxicity from prior therapy that has not resolved to Grade ≤ 1 baseline (exceptions: alopecia or skin hyperpigmentation; fatigue and peripheral neuropathy must have resolved to Grade ≤ 2).

Requirement for chronic systemic glucocorticoids exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents (or equivalent immunosuppression) within the preceding two years (physiologic hormone replacement exempt).
Inability or unwillingness to comply with protocol requirements.

Prior treatment required: Minimum two prior systemic therapy lines for Waldenström macroglobulinemia (exposure to Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor permissible or patient deemed unsuitable for BTK‐targeted therapy). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Current untreated or uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus infection.
• Positive hepatitis B surface antigen; detectable hepatitis B DNA; or unresolved hepatitis C viremia.
• Chronic liver disease classified as Child–Pugh grade B or C.
• Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to conceive during study participation and up to 90 days post‑treatment (breastfeeding restriction extends to 2 weeks).
• Central nervous system involvement by Waldenström macroglobulinemia.
• Active alcohol dependence or illicit substance misuse.
• Concomitant use of moderate or strong cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers.
• Enrollment in another interventional therapeutic clinical trial.
• Past malignant neoplasms except adequately treated basal or squamous cell skin carcinoma, cervical intra‑epithelial neoplasia, superficial urothelial carcinoma, localized prostate cancer, or other solid tumors in long‑term remission.
• Clinically significant acute or chronic illnesses—including active infections necessitating antimicrobial therapy—that could compromise safety or data interpretation.
• Inability to ingest oral capsules.
• Major cardiovascular disorders: unstable angina, myocardial infarction within six months, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤45 % (within past year), New York Heart Association class III‑IV heart failure, uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, or resting QTc interval >480 msec.
• Ongoing active hemorrhage requiring transfusion or invasive management.

Prior treatment required: Must have received prior CNS‑directed therapy (chemotherapy and/or radiotherapy) leading to initial response. Prior treatment excluded: Prior anti‑Programmed Death‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2 agents; prior agents targeting CTLA‑4, OX‑40, CD137; progression on ibrutinib or any Bruton’s tyrosine kinase inhibitor; receipt of high‑dose systemic corticosteroids (dexamethasone >4 mg/day or equivalent for more than 3 continuous days) within 2 weeks preceding registration. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients unable to undergo magnetic resonance imaging of the brain.
History of (non‑infectious) pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis.
Clinically significant cardiovascular disease such as uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias, congestive heart failure greater than New York Heart Association Class II, unstable angina, or myocardial infarction within six months of screening; uncontrolled hypertension despite optimal medical management.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus with glycated haemoglobin >8%.
Severe hepatic dysfunction outside predefined laboratory parameters.
Renal impairment beyond allowable creatinine clearance or serum creatinine limits.
Active bleeding diathesis (e.g., von Willebrand disease, hemophilia).
Recent arterial thromboembolic event (stroke/TIA) within three months.
Concurrent use of warfarin or vitamin K antagonist derivatives within seven days of initiating study treatment.
Use of concomitant strong/moderate CYP3A4/5 enzyme inhibitors or inducers that cannot be stopped appropriately.
Requirement for ongoing high‑dose systemic glucocorticoids (greater than prednisolone/prednisone 10 mg/day equivalence).
Live viral vaccination within thirty days prior to first dose of study medication.
Presence of another actively treated malignant neoplasm progressing or requiring therapy within the past three years (excluding certain cured skin carcinomas and carcinoma in situ).
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or acquired immunodeficiency syndrome.
Chronic active hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).
Active tuberculosis infection.
Immunodeficient state or chronic systemic steroid usage exceeding ten milligrams prednisone-equivalent daily.
Previous allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Significant psychiatric disorder or substance misuse impairing compliance with study protocols.
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant or sire a child during the study period extending from screening until 120 days after the last administered dose.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥28 days to <18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Any disease (benign or malignant) treated with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Recipient of allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant with a diagnosis of HSCT‑associated thrombotic microangiopathy (platelet count < 50,000/mm³ **or** ↓ ≥ 50 % from post‑transplant peak **and** evidence of microangiopathic hemolysis [schistocytes present **or** LDH > ULN **or** haptoglobin < LLN]); must demonstrate at least one high‑risk HSCT‑TMA feature (spot urinary protein/creatinine ratio > 2 mg/mg **or** serum creatinine > 1.5 × pre‑TMA baseline **or** biopsy‑confirmed gastrointestinal TMA **or** TMA‑related neurologic deficit **or** unexplained pericardial/pleural effusion **or** unexplained pulmonary hypertension **or** Grade III–IV graft‑versus‑host disease **or** serum C5b‑9 > 244 ng/mL). Prior treatment required: receipt of an allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Prior treatment excluded: eculizumab **or** ravulizumab **or** defibrotide. Biomarkers required: platelet count < 50,000/mm³ **or** ≥ 50 % reduction from peak **and** microangiopathic hemolysis indicated by schistocytes **or** LDH > ULN **or** haptoglobin < LLN. Biomarkers excluded: ADAMTS13 activity < 10 % **or** stool/blood PCR-positive shiga‑toxin producing E.coli/HUS **or** HIV positivity.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Severe uncontrolled bacterial, fungal, or viral infection requiring therapeutic antimicrobials.  
Malignant hypertension (BP > 99th percentile + 5 mm Hg with retinal hemorrhage or cotton‑wool spots).  
Prognosis indicating life expectancy <3 months unrelated to HSCT‑TMA.  
Pregnant or lactating individuals.  
Alanine transaminase or Aspartate transaminase >5 times upper limit of normal.  
Human immunodeficiency virus infection confirmed by antigen/PCR assay.  
Employment relationship between participant (or their parent/legal guard) and sponsor, CRO, investigators, or study personnel.  
Known hypersensitivity to any component of the study drug.  
Any additional medical condition deemed by the investigator to place the patient at undue risk.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: glioblastoma (World Health Organization grade IV glioma). Histology allowed: glioblastoma, IDH‑wildtype (absence of IDH‑1 or IDH‑2 mutation). Cancer burden allowed: first recurrence or second recurrence after prior radiation therapy AND measurable contrast‑enhancing tumor meeting RAN​O criteria. Prior treatment required: prior external beam radiation therapy (at least 12 weeks ago). Prior treatment excluded: NA. general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients who have been treated with bevacizumab (unless intra‑arterial use approved).  
Patients who have not fully recovered (> Grade 1) from adverse events related to prior therapy (excluding alopecia and fatigue).  
Subjects requiring escalated or chronic supra‑physiological glucocorticoid doses (>10 mg/day prednisone equivalents or >2 mg dexamethasone) for disease control at registration.  
Patients currently receiving or having previously received an immune checkpoint inhibitor.  
Individuals with documented immunodeficiency, including human immunodeficiency virus/AIDS.  
Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg positive) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).  
History of active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.  
Concurrent progressive secondary malignant neoplasm needing active treatment (exceptions: cured cutaneous basal/squamous cell carcinoma, in‑situ cervical cancer).  
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the preceding 2 years (disease‑modifying agents, systemic steroids, or immunosuppressants).  
Any history or evidence of active non‑infectious pneumonitis.  
Active infections necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.  
Uncontrolled concurrent medical conditions, lab abnormalities, or concomitant medications that could compromise trial integrity or participant safety.  
Psychiatric disorder or substance misuse interfering with compliance.  
Pregnancy, lactation, or intention to become pregnant/father a child during the study window up to 120 days after last dose.  
Incapacity to undergo gadolinium‑enhanced magnetic resonance imaging of the brain (e.g., implanted devices incompatible with MRI, severe contrast allergies).  
Receipt of a live viral vaccination within 30 days before initiating study drug (live nasal influenza vaccine prohibited; inactivated vaccinations permitted).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Any malignancy that has been treated with allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation. Biomarkers excluded: Documented Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection; active hepatitis B virus infection requiring treatment or hepatitis B surface antigen positivity or anti‑hepatitis B core antibody positivity with detectable DNA indicating risk for reactivation; active hepatitis C virus infection requiring treatment.
general_exclusion_criteria: Active uncontrolled systemic infection; known human immunodeficiency virus infection; active hepatitis B or C infection requiring treatment or placing subject at risk for viral reactivation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of severe brain injury or stroke.
Any seizure history unrelated to the tumor.
Treatment with strong cytochrome P450 3A4 enzyme inhibitors or inducers within two weeks prior to study drug initiation.
Concurrent use of seizurogenic medication.
Previous interstitial lung disease, drug‑induced interstitial lung disease, or radiation pneumonitis requiring steroids.
Severe and/or uncontrolled comorbid medical conditions (e.g., severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease).
Prior exposure to known pulmonotoxic chemotherapeutics (busulfan, bleomycin) resulting in pulmonary toxicity.
Historical pulmonary toxicity from nitrosourea compounds when associated with lung damage.
History or current presence of myopathy or serum creatine kinase > five times the upper limit of normal on two occasions.
Recent myocardial infarction (<six months), New York Heart Association class II–IV congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or serious cardiac arrhythmias.
Evidence of active severe infectious processes—including tuberculosis, untreated hepatitis B or C (as noted for Japan cohort)—at screening.
Unresolved adverse events graded higher than NCI CTCAE version 4.03 grade 1 at baseline; persistent grade 2 toxicities may preclude participation.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Major concurrent illness or organ dysfunction—including plasma cell leukemia, Waldenström’s macroglobulinemia, POEMS syndrome, primary AL amyloidosis.

Active infections requiring ongoing antimicrobial therapy.

Uncontrolled psychiatric or substance-use disorders impairing compliance.  
Legal incapacity or lack of sufficient legal authority to give consent.  
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Concurrent administration of other cancer‑specific therapy or investigational agents during the study.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness including active infections, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, or cardiac arrhythmia.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Presence of a second malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment (exceptions noted for certain skin cancers, in‑situ cervical cancer, and low‑risk prostate cancer).
Significant preexisting autoimmune disorders; prolonged (>4 weeks) systemic steroid use greater than prednisone 10 mg/day or equivalent.
Major surgical procedure or serious trauma within 4 weeks preceding enrollment or planned within 4 weeks after first dose.
History of bleeding tendency or coagulopathy, clinically significant hemorrhage, or need for therapeutic anticoagulation within 14 days prior to therapy.

Prior treatment excluded: Receipt of any anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, anti‑PD‑L2, CTLA‑4, OX‑40 or CD137 agent, or any prior chemotherapy or radiotherapy directed to primary central nervous system lymphoma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Cannot undergo magnetic resonance imaging.
Involvement solely of ocular tissue without concomitant brain or spinal cord disease.
Concurrent participation in another interventional investigative study.
History of Grade ≥3 allergic or hypersensitivity reaction to pembrolizumab, high‑dose methotrexate, temozolomide, rituximab, or their excipients.
Active autoimmune disease that has required systemic immunosuppression (excluding physiological hormone replacements) within the preceding 2 years.
Administration of a live viral vaccine within 30 days before first dose of study medication.
Presence of a second actively progressing malignancy or one requiring systemic therapy within the past 3 years (exceptions noted for cured cutaneous carcinomas and carcinoma in situ).
Previous non‑infectious pneumonitis necessitating steroids or existing pneumonitis.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus with glycated haemoglobin > 8%.
Inability to ingest oral capsule formulation or significant gastrointestinal impairment (e.g., malabsorptive syndromes, gastrectomy, extensive bowel resections, complete obstruction).
Use of moderate or strong CYP3A4/5 enzyme inhibitors or inducers concurrently with study drugs.
Requirement for enzyme‑inducing anticonvulsants (which must be replaced prior to enrolment).
Confirmed chronic hepatitis B infection (positive HBsAg) or active hepatitis C infection (detectable HCV RNA).
Active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Underlying immunodeficiency or chronic systemic glucocorticoid therapy exceeding 10 mg/day prednisone equivalents.
History of allogenic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Clinically significant third‑space fluids (large pleural effusions, ascites, or generalized oedema).
Severe psychiatric or substance‑use disorder impairing compliance with protocol procedures.
Pregnancy, lactation, intention to become pregnant, or unwillingness to employ approved contraception throughout treatment and for 120 days thereafter.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, or pulmonary fibrosis.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure NYHA Class III-IV, recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, arrhythmias requiring intervention.
Significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine >2× ULN or estimated GFR <30 mL/min/1.73 m²).
Severe hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin >2× ULN, AST/ALT >5× ULN).
Active central nervous system involvement or untreated/uncontrolled brain metastasis.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection, chronic hepatitis B or C infection with detectable viral load.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another investigational therapeutic study.

general_exclusion_criteria: History of significant untreated cardiovascular disease, uncontrolled arrhythmia, congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction within six months, or clinically meaningful instability of cardiac function. Active uncontrolled infections including bacterial, fungal, tuberculous, opportunistic, HIV infection with detectable viral load or CD4<200, chronic hepatitis B or C meeting predefined criteria, or active cytomegalovirus infection requiring therapy. Recent (<6 weeks) administration of live attenuated vaccines. Chronic liver disease, veno‑occlusive disease, or current excessive alcohol intake. Significant baseline renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min) or hepatic insufficiency exceeding protocol limits. Central nervous system or leptomeningeal involvement by lymphoma causing symptomatic disease. Current use of systemic glucocorticoids >20 mg prednisone daily or other immunosuppressive drugs. Pregnancy or breastfeeding; females of childbearing potential not employing approved contraceptive measures. Inability or refusal to comply with mandatory antimicrobial prophylaxis for Pneumocystis jirovecii pneumonia, HSV/VZV. Concomitant medication strongly inducing or inhibiting CYP3A enzymes that cannot be safely held. Prior allogenic stem cell transplantation within six months or active graft‑versus‑host disease necessitating immunosuppression. Prior therapy with duvelisib or ruxolitinib halted due to toxicities. Major surgical procedures or gastrointestinal anatomy altering drug absorption (gastric bypass, total gastrectomy) deemed unsuitable. Concurrent second active malignancy requiring therapy, excluding adequately treated early‑stage non‑melanoma skin cancers. Severe uncontrolled concomitant illnesses judged by investigator to pose unacceptable risk.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another prior invasive breast cancer (except DCIS >5 years ago).
Other prior invasive malignancies require being disease‐free ≥5 years.
Chronic high‑dose systemic corticosteroid use (>specified doses) or other immunosuppressants.
QTc prolongation >480 ms.
Uncontrolled serious medical conditions (severe auto­immune disease, uncontrolled arrhythmia aside from stable AFib, recent (<6 months) acute coronary events, NYHA class II or higher heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension).
Active or untreated pneumonia/pneumonitis, interstitial lung disease, DLCO <50 %, resting oxygen sat ≤88%.
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.
Severe active infections.
Significant liver disease (cirrhosis, chronic active/hepatitis B/C).
HIV infection requiring antiretroviral therapy.
Gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, uncontrolled nausea/vomit/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).
Bleeding diatheses or therapeutic anticoagulation.
Retinal pathology (retinitis pigmentosa, retinal vein occlusion, macular degeneration affecting vision).
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or lack of adequate contraception in persons of childbearing potential.
