Prior treatment required: • maximum two prior systemic regimens for endometrial carcinoma, INCLUDING at least one platinum‑containing chemotherapy line **AND** at least one anti‑programmed death‑protein‑1/programmed death‑ligand‑1 antibody therapy (either alone or in combination). Prior treatment excluded: prior EZH2 inhibitor. Biomarkers required: ARID1A wildtype (absence of pathogenic ARID1A alteration by NGS). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; Symptomatic untreated brain metastases or unresolved CNS involvement; Significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, stroke, unstable angina, NYHA class III-IV congestive heart failure, serious ventricular arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension); Baseline prolonged QTc (>480 ms for most cohorts, >450 ms for cohorts M7/M8); Recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or incomplete recovery from surgery; Severe gastrointestinal diseases affecting oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, malabsorptive syndromes, refractory nausea/vomiting, extensive resections); Active uncontrolled infection needing IV antibiotics within 14 days; Past or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease; Concurrent second malignancy except appropriately treated localized skin cancers, cervix intraepithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, indolent/asymptomatic prostate cancer, or cancers in remission ≥3 years; History of T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma/leukemia; Chronic active Hepatitis B/C or HIV infection; Clinically active viral hepatitis or chronic liver disease; Poor performance status (ECOG >1); Pregnant or lactating women and individuals not employing contraception when applicable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate gland. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma of the prostate (metastatic castration‑resistant). Cancer burden allowed: measurable soft‑tissue disease per PCWG3; metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: • progression on abiraterone while receiving castration (**required**) **and** no more than one prior taxane‑based chemotherapy regimen in the mCRPC or hormone‑sensitive setting. Prior treatment excluded: prior ezh2 inhibitor; prior enzalutamide (any line). Biomarkers required: baseline testosterone ≤50 ng/dL and continued surgical or medical castration. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; Symptomatic untreated brain metastases or unresolved CNS involvement; Significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, stroke, unstable angina, NYHA class III-IV congestive heart failure, serious ventricular arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension); Baseline prolonged QTc (>480 ms for most cohorts, >450 ms for cohorts M7/M8); Recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or incomplete recovery from surgery; Severe gastrointestinal diseases affecting oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, malabsorptive syndromes, refractory nausea/vomiting, extensive resections); Active uncontrolled infection needing IV antibiotics within 14 days; Past or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease; Concurrent second malignancy except appropriately treated localized skin cancers, cervix intraepithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, indolent/asymptomatic prostate cancer, or cancers in remission ≥3 years; History of T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma/leukemia; Chronic active Hepatitis B/C or HIV infection; Clinically active viral hepatitis or chronic liver disease; Poor performance status (ECOG >1); Pregnant or lactating women and individuals not employing contraception when applicable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: male. Cancer type allowed: prostate gland. Histology allowed: adenocarcinoma of the prostate (metastatic castration‑resistant). Cancer burden allowed: measurable soft‑tissue disease per PCWG3; metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: • progression on abiraterone while receiving castration (**required**) **and** no more than one prior taxane‑based chemotherapy regimen in the hormone‑sensitive setting. Prior treatment excluded: prior ezh2 inhibitor; prior enzalutamine, apalutamide, darolutamide, or any investigative androgen‑receptor pathway inhibitor. Biomarkers required: baseline testosterone ≤50 ng/dL and continued surgical or medical castration. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of solid organ or allogenic stem cell transplantation; Symptomatic untreated brain metastases or unresolved CNS involvement; Significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, stroke, unstable angina, NYHA class III-IV congestive heart failure, serious ventricular arrhythmias, uncontrolled hypertension); Baseline prolonged QTc (>480 ms for most cohorts, >450 ms for cohorts M7/M8); Recent major surgery (<4 weeks) or incomplete recovery from surgery; Severe gastrointestinal diseases affecting oral drug absorption (ulcerative colitis, malabsorptive syndromes, refractory nausea/vomiting, extensive resections); Active uncontrolled infection needing IV antibiotics within 14 days; Past or current pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease; Concurrent second malignancy except appropriately treated localized skin cancers, cervix intraepithelial neoplasia, superficial bladder cancer, indolent/asymptomatic prostate cancer, or cancers in remission ≥3 years; History of T‑cell lymphoblastic lymphoma/leukemia; Chronic active Hepatitis B/C or HIV infection; Clinically active viral hepatitis or chronic liver disease; Poor performance status (ECOG >1); Pregnant or lactating women and individuals not employing contraception when applicable.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease with measurable disease (serum M‑protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL, or urinary M‑protein ≥ 200 mg/24h, or serum free light chain > 100 mg/L with abnormal kappa/lambda ratio). Prior treatment required: lenalidomide **and** anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody (minimum 2 cycles, unless discontinued early owing to intolerance) **and** at least one prior line of anti‑myeloma therapy resulting in minimal response or better. Prior treatment excluded: prior mezigdomide **or** prior carfilzomib **or** any prior allogeneic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease,
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction or symptomatic heart failure,
Significant renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 ml/min),
Severe hepatic impairment (bilirubin >​3× ULN or AST/ALT >​5× ULN),
Active central nervous system malignancy or untreated/progressive brain metastases,
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or chronic hepatitis B or C infection requiring ongoing antiviral therapy,
Concurrent serious infections,
Pregnancy or breastfeeding,
Performance status worse than Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group score 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: breast cancer. Histology allowed: invasive breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: stage I, stage II, or stage III (local or regional disease, no distant metastasis). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: any endocrine therapy, chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or investigational therapy for the current breast cancer diagnosis. Biomarkers required: hormone receptor positive (estrogen receptor > 1% **or** progesterone receptor > 1%) **and** HER2 negative (as defined by ASCO/CAP guidelines). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: severe cardiac disease including congestive heart failure NYHA class III or greater; significant pulmonary disease or severe respiratory illness; uncontrolled infections; interstitial lung disease or severe dyspnea at rest requiring supplemental oxygen; severe renal impairment (estimated creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min); extensive gastrointestinal surgical resections affecting stomach or small intestine; active inflammatory bowel disease (Crohn’s disease or ulcerative colitis) leading to grade 2 or worse diarrhea; active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or clinically evident viral infection (HIV positivity, active hepatitis B or C); history of syncope due to cardiovascular cause, pathogenic ventricular arrhythmias (ventricular tachycardia, ventricular fibrillation), or sudden cardiac arrest.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Multiple Myeloma. Histology allowed: Plasma cell neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with measurable disease per International Myeloma Working Group criteria after receiving ≥3 prior lines of therapy including a proteasome inhibitor, an immunomodulatory drug, and an anti‑CD38 antibody. Prior treatment required: Proteasome inhibitor AND Immunomodulatory agent AND Anti‑CD38 antibody. Prior treatment excluded: Anti‑GPRC5D agent OR MMAE‑containing regimen OR Allogeneic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Participants exhibiting clinical signs of central nervous system involvement of multiple myeloma.
Known chronic obstructive pulmonary disease or history of interstitial lung disease.
Moderate or severe persistent asthma within the past five years or uncontrolled asthma of any severity.
Severe cardiovascular disease that is not adequately controlled.
History of immunodeficiency disease.
Peripheral neuropathy grade ≥ 2.
Primary refractory multiple myeloma.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Solid malignant neoplasms. Histology allowed: Any solid‑tumour histology. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable disease (advanced). Prior treatment required: Documentation of progression on ≥1 prior standard antitumour therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Inactivating ATM mutation **or** MYC amplification **or** FBXW7 mutation **or** CCNE1 amplification **or** Mutation of a SWI/SNF complex member (ARID1A, PBRM1, SMARCA4, ARID2, ARID1B, SMARCA2, SS18) **or** Alteration of ATRX or DAXX. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Allergic reaction attributable to peposertib, M1774 or structurally similar substances; inability to permanently discontinue strong CYP3A4/5, CYP2C19, CYP2C9 modulators or prohibited P‑gp/BCRP/OCT1/OATP substrate medicines; uncontrolled intercurrent medical illness (ongoing severe infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, persistent drainage devices); baseline corrected QT interval > 470 ms; clinically significant cardiac disease exceeding NYHA functional classification 2B; pregnancy or lactation; inability to comply with contraceptive requirements; receipt of additional investigational agent concurrently; residual toxicities greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia, stable endocrinopathies ≤Grade 2, skin toxicity ≤Grade 2).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: non small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: non squamous (including mixed histology with squamous component). Cancer burden allowed: Stage IIIB or IIIC unresectable disease or Stage IV disease (advanced/metastatic) with measurable lesions per RECIST v1.1. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior administration of any topoisomerase I‑targeting cytotoxic agent, prior TROP2‑targeted therapy, prior anti‑programmed death‑1, anti‑programmed death‑ligand‑1, anti‑programmed death‑ ligand‑2 therapy, or any other immune checkpoint inhibitor. Biomarkers required: Programmed death‑receptor‑1 tumour proportion score ≥50% (high PD‑L1 expression). Biomarkers excluded: Absence of actionable genomic alterations—including EGFR activating mutations, ALK rearrangements, ROS1 rearrangements, NTRK alterations, BRAF V600 mutations/rearrangements, RET rearrangements, MET amplifications or exon‑14 skipping events.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease (including radiation‑induced) requiring steroids, or unresolved/current ILD/pneumonitis.
Active, untreated central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; symptomatic brain metastases; unstable intracranial disease (participants with previously treated, asymptomatic, radiographically stable brain metastases are permitted).
Recent thoracic radiotherapy meeting any of: less than 4 weeks between last session and cycle 1 day 1, or total lung irradiation exceeding 30 Gray within 6 months preceding treatment initiation.
Uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease: mean QTcF >470 ms, myocardial infarction within 6 months, uncontrolled angina within 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction <50%, New York Heart Association class II-IV congestive heart failure, uncontrolled hypertension (SBP >180 mmHg or DBP >110 mmHg). Patients with prior class II‑IV heart failure must return to class I and achieve LVEF ≥50% before enrollment.
Clinically severe pulmonary impairment due to preexisting lung diseases, autoimmune/connective‑tissue disorders involving lungs, or prior complete pneumonectomy.
Significant corneal disease.
Receipt of a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within 30 days before first dose (mRNA COVID‑19 vaccines exempt).
Active, poorly controlled autoimmune disease that required systemic therapy within the past two years.
Immunodeficiency syndromes or chronic systemic corticosteroid use >10 mg/day prednisone equivalents (or other immunosuppression) within 7 days prior to dosing.
Untreated or inadequately controlled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection, active Hepatitis B or C infection, or any uncontrolled infectious process necessitating intravenous antimicrobial therapy.
Allogeneic stem cell or solid organ transplantation.
Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status worse than 0 or 1.
Life expectancy shorter than three months.
Inadequate hematologic, hepatic, or renal laboratory values according to protocol specifications.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Solid tumor (any primary site). Histology allowed: Any solid‑tumor histology. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable, and/or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: KRAS G12C or G12D or G12V or G12A or G12S or G13D mutation detected in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease; Presence of unresolved adverse events higher than Grade 1 (except alopecia, hearing loss, peripheral neuropathy, stable endocrine disorders); Clinically significant cardiovascular disease including unstable angina, acute coronary syndromes, recent myocardial infarction, reduced left‑ventricular ejection fraction or congestive heart failure, uncontrolled or symptomatic arrhythmias; Active chronic hepatitis B or C infection or untreated human immunodeficiency virus infection; Concurrent active malignancy requiring therapy (excluding cases in remission with anticipated survival longer than two years); Ongoing uncontrolled systemic bacterial, viral, fungal, or parasitic infection; Past or current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis deemed clinically significant.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Invasive breast carcinoma (all subtypes). Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable or metastatic disease with measurable lesion(s) per RECIST v1.1; patients with treated or clinically inactive central nervous system metastases are acceptable. Prior treatment required: Documented prior exposure to trastuzumab deruxtecan with subsequent disease progression on or intolerance to that agent; candidate must be eligible to receive one of the physician’s choice chemotherapy agents (eribulin **or** gemcitabine **or** vinorelbine **or** capecitabine). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: HER2‐positive status confirmed by central laboratory testing according to ASCO‑CAP guidelines (assessed at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Has known or suspected leptomeningeal disease.
Has uncontrolled or significant cardiovascular disease.
Has residual toxicity from prior cancer therapy greater than Grade 1 (except where protocol permits).
Requires ongoing intravenous antimicrobial, antiviral, or antifungal therapy for uncontrolled infection.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Active hepatitis B or C infection.
Active severe acute respiratory syndrome coronavirus 2 infection.
History of life‑threatening hypersensitivity reaction to monoclonal antibodies, recombinant proteins, or excipients in zanidatamab formulation.
Contraindication to receiving trastuzumab.
Serious underlying medical or psychiatric disorder compromising safe participation.
Inability to safely administer the selected physician’s choice chemotherapy.
Any condition deemed by investigators to preclude enrollment or confound outcomes.
Previous allogenic bone marrow, stem cell, or solid organ transplantation.
Recent (within four weeks) major surgery or traumatic injury.
Hypersensitivity to any component of the study medications, including chemotherapy.
Pregnancy or current lactation; plans for future conception without appropriate contraception measures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Adenocarcinoma of the prostate. Histology allowed: Pure adenocarcinoma (no neuroendocrine component). Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic castration‑resistant prostate cancer with at least one metastatic lesion detectable on CT/MRI/bone scan and documented disease progression per PCWG3 criteria (either rising PSA, soft‑tissue enlargement/new lesions, or development of ≥2 new bone lesions). Prior treatment required: Prior surgical or medical castration (orchiectomy and/or continual androgen‑deprivation therapy resulting in castrate testosterone levels) and documented progression on at least one androgen‑receptor directed therapy (enzalutamide **or** abiraterone **or** apalutamide **or** darolutamide) and receipt of exactly one taxane chemotherapy agent in the metastatic castration‑resistant setting (docetaxel **or** cabazitaxel). Prior treatment excluded: Six transmembrane epithelial antigen of the prostate 1 (STEAP1)‑targeted therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of symptomatic central nervous system metastasis (treated, asymptomatic, stable dural metastases are allowable); unresolved toxicities from prior anticancer therapy persisting at grade > 1 (with the exceptions noted for alopecia or stable, well‑controlled side effects).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Oligometastatic disease with 1 to 5 PSMA‑positive metastatic lesions on PSMA PET/CT, at least one lesion fulfilling AJCC 8th ed M1 designation (distal lymph node, bone, or viscus), no liver or brain metastases, no confirmatory conventional imaging showing additional M1 disease, occurrence after definitive local therapy (not de novo oligometastatic presentation). Prior treatment required: Completed definitive therapy to the prostate comprising either radical prostatectomy (with or without postoperative radiation to prostate bed/pelvic nodes) OR external beam radiation therapy to the prostate (with or without seminal vesicle and/or pelvic nodal irradiation) OR low‑dose rate brachytherapy, performed prior to enrolment. Prior treatment excluded: Current androgen‑deprivation therapy producing serum testosterone ≤100 ng/dL, castration‑resistant prostate cancer, any systemic therapy instituted for metastatic disease—including androgen‑deprivation therapy or androgen‑receptor pathway inhibitors intended for metastatic disease, taxane or other chemotherapy for metastatic disease, radiopharmaceutical agents (e.g., strontium‑89, PSMA‑targeted radionuclides), immunotherapies (e.g., sipuleucel‑T), poly(ADP‑ribose) polymerase inhibitors, other FDA‑approved or investigational antitumour agents; hormonal agents used for prostate cancer indication other than benign prostatic hyperplasia (e.g., estrogens, 5‑α‑reductase inhibitors, steroidogenesis inhibitors). Biomarkers required: Demonstrated PSMA‑positive uptake on PSMA PET/CT for each qualifying lesion, assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of severe pulmonary toxicity or pneumonitis; symptomatic congestive heart failure or left ventricular ejection fraction <50%; creatinine clearance <30 ml/min or dialysis dependence; Child-Pugh class C liver impairment; untreated or unstable central nervous system metastases; active infection with HIV, Hepatitis B, or Hepatitis C; prolonged corrected QT interval or other clinically significant electrocardiac conduction disorders (e.g., sustained ventricular tachyarrhythmia, grade II/III AV block without pacing); Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status greater than 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic or locally advanced disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: EGFR activating mutation detected (e.g., exon 19 deletion or L858R) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, or unable to rule out pulmonary infiltrates on imaging; spinal cord compression or prior leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus; brain metastases unless previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and off high-dose corticosteroids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) for at least 4 weeks before enrollment; clinically significant cardiovascular disease as defined in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: non‑small cell lung cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: EGFR wild‑type (no activating EGFR mutation) assessed during screening. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, or unable to rule out pulmonary infiltrates on imaging; spinal cord compression or prior leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus; brain metastases unless previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and off high-dose corticosteroids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) for at least 4 weeks before enrollment; clinically significant cardiovascular disease as defined in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: head and neck cancer. Histology allowed: squamous cell carcinoma of the oral cavity. Cancer burden allowed: recurrent or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, or unable to rule out pulmonary infiltrates on imaging; spinal cord compression or prior leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus; brain metastases unless previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and off high-dose corticosteroids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) for at least 4 weeks before enrollment; clinically significant cardiovascular disease as defined in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: colorectal cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of non‑infectious interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis requiring steroids, current interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, or unable to rule out pulmonary infiltrates on imaging; spinal cord compression or prior leptomeningeal carcinomatosis; active infection including tuberculosis, hepatitis B virus, hepatitis C virus, or human immunodeficiency virus; brain metastases unless previously treated, asymptomatic, stable, and off high-dose corticosteroids (>10 mg prednisolone daily) for at least 4 weeks before enrollment; clinically significant cardiovascular disease as defined in the protocol.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: relapsed or refractory disease per IMWG criteria with measurable disease (Serum M‑protein ≥0.5 g/dL or Urine monoclonal protein ≥200 mg/24 h or Serum free light chain ≥10 mg/dL with abnormal ratio). Prior treatment required: ≥2 prior lines of therapy and exposure to an immunomodulatory agent (e.g., lenalidomide or pomalidomide) and a proteasome inhibitor and an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody. Prior treatment excluded: prior iberdomide exposure or prior belantamab mafodotin exposure or any prior BCMA‑directed therapy or prior allogeneic stem cell transplant. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of uncontrolled human immunodeficiency virus infection; Active hepatitis B or C infection not meeting predefined controlled criteria; Significant cardiac disease including uncontrolled arrhythmias, recent myocardial infarction or coronary interventions within 3 months, congestive heart failure NYHA class III-IV, uncontrolled hypertension, QTc prolongation >480 ms, left ventricular ejection fraction <30%; Severe pulmonary disorders such as interstitial lung disease or pneumonitis; Clinically significant hepatic insufficiency (Child‑Pugh grade >1) or biliary obstruction; Renal failure requiring dialysis or CrCl <30 ml/min; Untreated or progressing central nervous system lesions/metastases; Current active bacterial, viral, or fungal infection; Recent major surgery (<2 weeks) or incomplete wound healing; Known allergy or hypersensitivity to study drugs or excipients; Pregnant or breastfeeding individuals; Concurrent active second malignancy expected to impact survival or assessments; Symptomatic AL amyloidosis, active POEMS syndrome, or overt plasma cell leukemia; Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers unable to be discontinued.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: cutaneous malignant melanoma. Histology allowed: melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: stage IIIB, stage IIIC, stage IIID, or stage IV (any resectable, borderline resectable, or unresectable disease). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior PD‑1 or PD‑L1 inhibitor therapy for metastatic melanoma; concomitant anti‑cancer agents, investigational anti‑cancer therapies, or immunosuppressive agents within 6 months; FLT3 mutation or amplification. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: FLT3 mutation or amplification.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Active brain metastases or leptomeningeal metastases.
History of severe allergic reaction attributable to any vaccine therapy.
Active, clinically significant autoimmune disease (exceptions not applied universally).
Systemic corticosteroid use greater than 10 mg prednisone-equivalent daily or other immunosuppressive medication within 14 days of dosing.
Positive laboratory evidence of active hepatitis B virus infection, hepatitis C virus infection, or human immunodeficiency virus infection/AIDs.
Known hypersensitivity to nivolumab, pembrolizumab, or CDX‑301.
Pregnancy or currently breastfeeding.
Planned major surgery unrelated to melanoma resection.
Invasive malignancy within the preceding three years (aside from stipulated low‑risk histories).
Being a prisoner or subject involuntarily confined.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: plasma cell malignancy consistent with multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: newly diagnosed disease with measurable disease (serum M‑protein ≥ 1.0 g/dL or urinary M‑protein ≥ 200 mg/24h, or involved–free light chain ≥ 10 mg/dL with abnormal κ/λ ratio) and intent to undergo high‑dose chemotherapy with autologous stem cell transplantation. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell therapy OR prior B‑cell maturation antigen‑directed therapy OR any prior anti‑myeloma therapy other than a short course of corticosteroids. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of significant cardiovascular disease including congestive heart failure.
Uncontrolled hypertension or clinically unstable cardiac conditions.
Baseline hepatic impairment (elevated transaminases greater than five times upper normal limits).
Renal insufficiency requiring dialysis or creatinine clearance less than 30 ml/min.
Active infection including unresolved viral hepatitis B/C, HIV/AIDS, or ongoing severe bacterial/fungal infections.
Recent major surgery (<28 days) or serious traumatic injury.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Concurrent participation in another interventional oncology trial.
Any condition compromising ability to comply with protocol procedures or assessments.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Small cell lung carcinoma. Histology allowed: Pure small cell lung carcinoma, combined small cell lung carcinoma, or mixed small cell lung carcinoma as defined by WHO 2021. Cancer burden allowed: Extensive‐stage (metastatic) or unresectable disease. Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of standard therapy for small cell lung carcinoma leading to disease progression, insufficient response, intolerance, or refusal of further standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of any cellular immunotherapy (e.g., CAR‑T) or gene therapy product, or prior DLL3‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: Availability of archived or fresh formalin‑filled, paraffin‑embedded tumor tissue (block or unstained slides) with accompanying pathology report for central testing. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior checkpoint inhibitor–associated pneumonitis  
• Clinically significant ascites, pleural or peritoneal effusions  
• Acquired or inheritable immunodeficiency lacking controllability  
• Leptomeningeal metastases  
• Active or symptomatic brain metastases (untreated)  
• Autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppressants (excluding physiological corticosteroid replacement or inhaled steroids)  
• Significant unmanaged cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, myocardial infarction ≤ 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 45%)  
• Other current malignant neoplasm (except permitted exceptions)  
• Severe uncontrolled infections (bacterial, viral, fungal)  
• Dependence on supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations  
• Dementia or altered mental status affecting participation  
• Recent (< 6 months) unresolved serious medical events  
• Untreated HIV, Hepatitis B, or Hepatitis C infection (unless meeting strict virologic/control criteria)  
• Hypersensitivity to study drug constituents (dimethyl sulfoxide, fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, tocilizumab)  
• Persistent (>Grade 1) adverse effects from preceding oncology therapies (aside from alopecia)  
• Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks before leukapheresis or scheduled within 4 weeks post‑infusion  
• Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive/father a child within 12 months after infusion  
• Prior allogenic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: Any. Cancer type allowed: Large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma. Histology allowed: Pure large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma or combined large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma as defined by WHO 2021. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced/unresectable disease (equivalent to extensive stage). Prior treatment required: At least one prior line of standard therapy for large cell neuroendocrine lung carcinoma resulting in disease progression, insufficient response, intolerance, or withdrawal of further standard therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Prior receipt of any cellular immunotherapy (e.g., CAR‑T) or gene therapy product, or prior DLL3‑directed therapy. Biomarkers required: Availability of archived or fresh formalin‑filled, paraffin‑embedded tumor tissue (block or unstained slides) with accompanying pathology report for central testing. Biomarkers excluded: None.
general_exclusion_criteria:
• Prior checkpoint inhibitor–associated pneumonitis  
• Clinically significant ascites, pleural or peritoneal effusions  
• Acquired or inheritable immunodeficiency lacking controllability  
• Leptomeningeal metastases  
• Active or symptomatic brain metastases (untreated)  
• Autoimmune diseases requiring systemic immunosuppressants (excluding physiological corticosteroid replacement or inhaled steroids)  
• Significant unmanaged cardiovascular disease (unstable angina, myocardial infarction ≤ 6 months, left ventricular ejection fraction < 45%)  
• Other current malignant neoplasm (except permitted exceptions)  
• Severe uncontrolled infections (bacterial, viral, fungal)  
• Dependence on supplemental oxygen to maintain saturations  
• Dementia or altered mental status affecting participation  
• Recent (< 6 months) unresolved serious medical events  
• Untreated HIV, Hepatitis B, or Hepatitis C infection (unless meeting strict virologic/control criteria)  
• Hypersensitivity to study drug constituents (dimethyl sulfoxide, fludarabine, cyclophosphamide, tocilizumab)  
• Persistent (>Grade 1) adverse effects from preceding oncology therapies (aside from alopecia)  
• Major surgical procedure within 4 weeks before leukapheresis or scheduled within 4 weeks post‑infusion  
• Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or plans to conceive/father a child within 12 months after infusion  
• Prior allogenic stem cell transplantation or solid organ transplantation.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: All ages. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Epstein‑Barr virus associated post‑transplant lymphoproliferative disease. Histology allowed: Not Burkitt lymphoma and not Classical Hodgkin lymphoma and not T‑cell lymphoma. Cancer burden allowed: Measurable systemic disease evidenced by FDG‑avid lesions (DeauVille score ≥ 3) on PET/CT (or MRI if indicated). general_exclusion_criteria:
Unresolved toxicities >grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia).
Concurrent enrolment in another investigational agent for AL amyloidosis.
Presence of active multiple myeloma meeting CRAB criteria.
Active clinically significant autoimmune disease.
Seropositivity for human immunodeficiency virus.
Severe uncontrolled orthostatic hypotension with syncope/presyncope despite optimal therapy.
Recent (<3 months) acute coronary syndrome or uncontrolled ventricular arrhythmia.
Left ventricular ejection fraction <30%.
Severe valvular stenosis (valve area <1.0 cm²) or severe congenital heart disease.
New York Heart Association class IV heart failure or acute decompensated congestion.
Planned major surgery requiring general anaesthesia within 4 weeks of screening or expected during study.
Corrected QT interval >550 ms on screening electrocardiograph absent a paced rhythm.
Screening ECG demonstrating acute myocardial infarction or non‑benign conduction abnormalities outside predefined benign patterns.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or intent to become pregnant during study or up to four months post‑treatment; inadequate contraception adherence.
Inability to comprehend or voluntarily sign informed consent.
Any medical, social, or psychological factor jeopardising safety or protocol compliance.
Plan for autologous stem cell transplant within the first six months of protocol therapy.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: adults (≥18 years). Sex allowed: both males and females. Cancer type allowed: any solid tumor. Histology allowed: any. Prior treatment required: relapse or progression after receiving (taxane **and** trastuzumab) **or** having received a pertuzumab ＋ trastuzumab‑based regimen as first‑line therapy for advanced disease **or** having received a trastuzumab deruxtecan‑based regimen. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicity or recent pneumonitis.
Active congestive heart failure, myocardial infarction, unstable angina, arrhythmia, or left ventricular ejection fraction <50%.
Serious uncontrolled infections, including human immunodeficiency virus, chronic hepatitis B or C infection.
Severe hepatic impairment (elevated bilirubin >3× upper normal limit or transaminases >5× ULN).
Significant renal dysfunction (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min).
Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system metastases requiring emergent therapeutic interventions.
Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to use adequate contraception.
Concurrent anti-cancer therapy outside the study drugs.
Inadequate bone marrow function (ANC <1500/mm³, platelets <100,000/mm³, hemoglobin <9 g/dL).

Recent peptic ulcer disease, inflammatory bowel disease flare, gastrointestinal perforation, or intra‑abdominal abscess within 4 weeks.

Prior treatment required: 1 to 3 prior lines of antimyelma therapy **and** prior lenalidomide exposure **and** attainment of Minimum Response or better to at least one prior antimyeloma therapy. Prior treatment excluded: prior mezigdomide **or** prior pomalidomide. Biomarkers required: measurable disease defined by serum M‑protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL or urine M‑protein ≥ 200 mg/24 hr or serum free light chain level > 100 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically significant pulmonary toxicities such as interstitial pneumonitis, recent myocardial infarction or unstable angina, congestive heart failure New York Heart Association class III-IV, left ventricular ejection fraction ≤40%, severe chronic obstructive pulmonary disease requiring oxygen supplementation, uncontrolled arrhythmias, grade ≥ 2 peripheral neuropathy, active systemic infections including viral hepatitis B/C, human immunodeficiency virus infection, untreated central nervous system lesions or symptomatic brain metastases, ongoing Grade ≥ 3 nausea/vomiting despite optimal supportive care, bilirubin >1.5× upper limit normal, AST/ALT >2.5× ULN, creatinine clearance <30 mL/min, platelet count <50 000/mm³, absolute neutrophil count <500/mm³, pregnant or breastfeeding women, inability to comply with study procedures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18. Sex allowed: Both. Cancer type allowed: Mantle cell lymphoma. Histology allowed: Mantle cell lymphoma (World Health Organization–defined). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed or refractory disease with measurably/evaluably persistent disease after at least one prior systemic therapy, including documented failure of a Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor. Prior treatment required: at least one prior line of systemic therapy for mantle cell lymphoma **and** prior Bruton's tyrosine kinase inhibitor therapy with disease progression during treatment or relapse/persistence after stopping the inhibitor or failure to attain a partial response after twelve weeks of therapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: CD20 expression on neoplastic cells if the patient has previously received CD20‑directed therapy. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Investigational anticancer agent or therapy within five half‑lives prior to study start (except ongoing BTKi allowable until one day pre‑treatment). Major surgery within four weeks preceding study start. Radiation therapy within seven days of starting study treatment. CAR‑T cell infusion within thirty days of Cycle 1 Day 1. Active hepatitis B infection (HBsAg+) or hepatitis B core antibody+/detectable HBV DNA; active hepatitis C infection (anti‑HCV + & HCV RNA+). Human immunodeficiency virus infection not meeting immune/compliance thresholds (CD4<250, AIDS‑defining OI <1 yr, unstable ART, VL≥400). Other active malignancies needing therapy within three years (exceptions noted). Pregnancy, breastfeeding, unwillingness to meet contraception/REMS requirements. Current serious infectious process requiring intravenous antibiotics or recent hospitalisation for infection (<4 weeks). Significant untreated liver disease or uncontrolled alcoholism. Active CNS lymphoma involvement (parenchymal or leptomeningeal). Grade ≥2 toxicities attributable to prior cancer therapy. Severe allergy/anaphylaxis to monoclonal antibodies or related biologics. Confirmed progressive multifocal leukoencephalopathy. Hypersensitivity to imide drugs or formulation components. Prior bispecific antibody targeting CD20×CD3. Prior lenalidomide or other IMiD usage within twelve months, or demonstrated lenalidomide resistance (no PR/CR within six months). Autologous stem cell transplant ≤3 months ago (requires full haematological recovery). Allogeneic stem cell transplant ≤3 months ago, active graft‑versus‑host disease, or ongoing immunosuppression for GVHD. High risk/history of thromboembolism refusing anticoagulation prophylaxis. Concomitant anticancer or experimental therapeutics. Live vaccines within twenty‑eight days of initiating study treatment. Systemic immunosuppressants within two weeks or five half‑lives before dosing. Recent systemic steroids exceeding stipulated limits (short courses >5 days prohibited; chronic ≧20 mg pred equivalents acceptable if stable ≥4 weeks). Uncontrolled comorbid conditions compromising safety or data integrity—including NYHA class III/IV heart failure, recent MI (<6 months), unstable arrhythmias/angina, severe COPD or asthma exacerbations. Chronic active Epstein‑Barr virus infection or reactivated EBV disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: multiple myeloma. Cancer burden allowed: relapsed and refractory disease after ≥4 prior lines of antimyeloma therapy and after idecabtagene vicleucel with at least a partial response, enrolment ≤90 days post‑infusion. Prior treatment required: immunomodulatory agent and proteasome inhibitor and anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody among prior lines; idecabtagene vicleucel administered per FDA‐approved labeling with documented partial response and infused ≤90 days before screening. Prior treatment excluded: prior iberdomide exposure. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of another malignancy within 3 years, excluding completed resections of basal cell carcinoma, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, in‑situ carcinomas, or low‑risk prostate cancer after definitive cure.
Active central nervous system involvement.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disorders—including uncontrolled hypertension, significant arrhythmias, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, grade 3 thromboembolic events, or myocardial infarction within the preceding six months.
Positive testing for active Human Immunodeficiency Virus, hepatitis B virus (detectable PCR), or hepatitis C virus infection.
Pregnancy or current nursing/lactation.
Ongoing graft‑versus‑host disease of any grade after allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.
Symptomatic AL amyloidosis or plasma cell leukemic transformation.
Presence of POEMS syndrome.
Recent untreated or unresolved serious bacterial/fungal infection requiring systemic antimicrobial therapy within seven days of planned study entry.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: progressive disease after prior therapy that meets IWCLL 2018 criteria for initiating treatment. Prior treatment required: previously received a fixed‑duration first‑line regimen consisting of venetoclax together with an anti‑CD20 monoclonal antibody (±an additional investigational or conventional drug) that resulted in a confirmed complete remission, complete remission with incomplete bone‑marrow recovery, partial remission, or nodular partial remission, **AND** greater than 24 months has elapsed between the last dose of venetoclax and documentation of disease progression. Prior treatment excluded: any intervening systemic therapy for chronic lymphocytic leukemia after completion of the aforementioned venetoclax‐based regimen. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of active infection including unresolved pneumonia or recent pulmonary toxicities.
Uncontrolled cardiovascular disease (e.g., congestive heart failure NYHA class III-IV).
Significant hepatic impairment (bilirubin >3× ULN or AST/ALT >5× ULN).
Severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Active central nervous system involvement or untreated/uncontrolled brain metastasis.
Known human immunodeficiency virus infection or active hepatitis B/C infection.
Concurrent malignancy requiring active therapy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Performance status worse than ECOG 2.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Prior treatment excluded: any intervening systematic therapy for chronic lymphocytic leukemia after completion of the aforementioned venetoclide‑based regimen. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: Adults (≥ 18 years). Sex allowed: Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced, unresectable, or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Presence of PI3KαH1047R mutation in tumor tissue or plasma; at least one prior line of hormonal therapy in the advanced/metastatic setting **and** at least one prior line of cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor in the advanced/metastatic setting. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: PI3KαH1047R mutation detectable in tumor tissue or circulating tumor DNA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Treatment with any investigational product or other anti‑cancer therapy within 14 days before first dose. Prior exposure to the PI3KαH1047R mutant‑selective inhibitor LOXO‑783. Known activating KRAS mutation (for applicable cohorts). Deleterious PTEN mutation or lack of PTEN protein expression by IHC. Recent major surgery (< 28 days) or radiotherapy (> 7 days). Active untreated central nervous system metastases. Systemic glucocorticoids > 10 mg prednisone equivalents at enrolment. Uncontrolled type 1 or type 2 diabetes mellitus. Pregnancy or breastfeeding. Concurrent active malignancy or prior malignancy diagnosed within 2 years. Clinically significant cardiovascular disease (recent myocardial infarction, symptomatic heart failure grade ≥ 2, serious arrhythmia, uncontrolled hypertension). Significant hepatic impairment, renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 60 mL/min), or hematologic abnormalities outside protocol limits. History of immune‑mediated pneumonitis. Human immunodeficiency virus infection with CD4 < 350 cells/µL, recent opportunistic infection, or unsuppressed viremia. Chronic hepatitis B surface antigen–positive or core‐antibody positive with detectable HBV DNA; chronic hepatitis C antibody positive with detectable HCV RNA unless cured. Congenital long QT syndrome or QTcF > 470 ms on screen ECG. Current use of proton‑pump inhibitors, potent/moderate UGT2B7 inhibitors or inducers, or sensitive substrate medicines of OAT1/OAT3/BCRP/OATP1B1 where interaction risk exists.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both males and females. Cancer type allowed: Multiple myeloma. Histology allowed: Malignant plasma cell neoplasm (multiple myeloma). Cancer burden allowed: Relapsed and refractory disease with measurable paraprotein (Serum M‐protein ≥ 0.5 g/dL **or** Urinary M‑spike ≥ 200 mg/24 h **or** Free light chain ≥ 100 mg/L with abnormal κ/λ ratio) after prior therapy and attainment of at least a Partial Response to idecabtagene vicleucel. Prior treatment required: Received idecabtagene vicleucel per label after ≥ four prior lines of therapy that included an immunomodulatory drug, a proteasome inhibitor, and an anti‑CD38 monoclonal antibody, and demonstrated at least a Partial Response to the CAR‑T product. Prior treatment excluded: Previous anti‑BCMA bispecific antibody therapy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: active hepatitis B, hepatitis C, SARS‑CoV‑2, HIV, or any untreated bacterial/fungal/viral infection; uncontrolled cardiovascular disease including recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, significant arrhythmias, thromboembolic/cerebrovascular events, prolonged QT interval; other active malignancy within three years except adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin cancer or carcinoma in situ; grade ≥ 2 persistent peripheral sensory or motor neuropathy; history of Guillain–Barré syndrome or grade ≥ 3 peripheral motor polyneuropathy; current pregnancy or lactation; known hypersensitivity to study drug components; simultaneous enrolment in another interventional oncology trial; inability to discontinue live attenuated vaccinations before study initiation; residual toxicities greater than grade 1 (except alopecia and stable peripheral neuropathy); recent major surgery or invasive procedures; inadequate organ function outside predefined limits.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: 5 to 17 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Classical Hodgkin lymphoma. Histology allowed: Nodular sclerosing, Mixed cellularity, Lymphocyte rich, Lymphocyte depleted, or not otherwise specified. Cancer burden allowed: Ann Arbor stage I or II disease (early stage, non‑bulky or bulky as measured). Prior treatment required: Newly diagnosed untreated disease (must not have received any chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or antibody‑based therapy for classic Hodgkin lymphoma). Prior treatment excluded: Any prior chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or antibody‑based treatment directed at classic Hodgkin lymphoma. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Patients with nodular lymphocyte‑predominant Hodgkin lymphoma.
History of active interstitial pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Poorly controlled inherited or acquired immunodeficiency requiring ongoing systemic medication (e.g., primary immunodeficiency syndromes, organ transplant recipient).
Uncontrolled intercurrent serious illness (infection, severe autoimmune disorder, significant cardiac arrhythmia, unstable angina, gastrointestinal obstruction preventing oral medication uptake).
Peripheral neuropathy greater than grade 1 at enrollment or known Charcot‑Marie‑Tooth disease.
Presence of a prior or concurrent malignancy that could confound safety/efficacy assessments.
Previous solid organ transplantation.
Previous allogeneic hematopoietic stem‑cell transplantation.
Receipt of a live vaccine within 30 days before initiation of protocol therapy.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding at the time of enrolment.
Inability or unwillingness to comply with mandated contraception requirements for participants of reproductive potential.

Untreated brain metastasis.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥70 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma (hormone receptor‑positive). Cancer burden allowed: Distant metastatic disease (any visceral or bone sites); central nervous system metastases permitted only if untreated lesions ≤1 cm not requiring immediate local therapy, or previously treated lesions that are clinically stable and not requiring steroids or enzyme‑inducing antiepileptics. Prior treatment required: Progressive disease after prior endocrine therapy and/or prior cyclin‑dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor (palbociclib or ribociclib) and/or prior chemotherapy. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Estrogen‑receptor positive and/or progesterone‑receptor positive by immunohistochemistry; HER2‑negative (immunohistochemistry score 0 or 1+, or 2+ with negative in‑situ hybridisation). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: Major surgery within 14 days prior to receipt of study drug or lack of full postoperative recovery.; Current use of prohibited concomitant medications that cannot be stopped seven days before study drug administration.; Participation in another investigational therapeutic trial.; Receipt of anticancer agents other than the prescribed study regimens, unless withdrawal from the study.; Concomitant immunosuppressive agents other than allowable corticosteroids (e.g., systemic cytochrome inhibitors such as cyclosporine or tacrolimus) when deemed risky.; Use of herbal supplements potentially interacting with study drug pharmacokinetics.; Known hypersensitivity to any components/excipients of abemaciclib.; Active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, active fungal infection, or detectable viral infection (known HIV positivity or active Hepatitis B/C).; Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (ulcerative disease, uncontrolled nausea/vomiting/diarrhea, malabsorptive syndromes, extensive small‑bowel resections).; Cardiovascular histories posing elevated risk (syncope of cardiac origin, pathologic ventricular arrhythmias, sustained VT/VF, sudden cardiac arrest).; Uncontrolled severe/comorbid medical illnesses judged unsafe for trial participation (e.g., chronic pancreatitis, chronic active hepatitis).; Inability to ingest oral tablets.; Severe respiratory conditions (interstitial lung disease, resting hypoxia requiring supplemental O₂, severe dyspnoea).; Renal insufficiency with calculated CrCl < 30 mL/min.; Pre‑existent Grade ≥ 2 diarrhea due to underlying colon pathology (Crohn’s disease, ulcerative colitis, post‑surgical changes).; History of non‑adherence to medical regimens.; Any prior malignant neoplasm diagnosed within two years preceding enrolment with existing disease, excluding adequately treated basal/squamous cell skin carcinomas, non‑melanoma skin cancer, or surgically cured cervical cancer.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male. Cancer type allowed: Prostate cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: High‑risk disease defined as any of — clinical stage cT3a‑cT3b, pretreatment prostate specific antigen >20 ng/mL (doubling value if on 5‑alpha reductase inhibitor), Gleason score 8‑10, pelvic lymph node short‑axis ≥1 cm — together with prostate gland volume <100 cc and no distant metastases (M0). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior radiotherapy to the prostate/pelvic area that would cause overlap of planned radiation fields; prior radical prostatectomy. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Uncontrolled cardiac disease (e.g., recent myocardial infarction, unstable angina, New York Heart Association Class III‑IV heart failure).
Severe hepatic impairment (Child-Pugh C or bilirubin >2 × institutional upper limit of normal).
Severe renal impairment (creatinine clearance <30 mL/min) or dialysis dependence.
Active uncontrolled infections, including HIV with low CD4 count or untreated hepatitis B/C with replicating virus.
Untreated or symptomatic central nervous system/metastatic brain lesions.
Pregnancy or breastfeeding (for women participants, although trial limited to males).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Complete remission with measurable residual disease (≤5 % blasts) detected by flow cytometry, next-generation sequencing or polymerase chain reaction **or** 5–19 % residual myeloblasts in bone marrow. Prior treatment required: Minimum one cycle of a hypomethylating agent (azacitidine or decitabine) concomitant with venetoclax. Prior treatment excluded: Prior allogeneic stem cell transplant, organ transplant, donor lymphocyte infusion, CAR‑T cell therapy, NK cell therapy. Biomarkers required: Presence of at least one 2022 ELN adverse‑risk abnormality — either adverse‑risk karyotype (t(6;9), DEK::NUP214; KMT2A‑rearranged; t(9;22) BCR::ABL1; t(8;16); inv(3) or t(3;3);−5 or del(5q);−7; complex/monosomal karyotype) **or** adverse‑risk mutation (mutated TP53, ASXL1, BCOR, EZH2, RUNX1, SF3B1, SRSF2, STAG2, U2AF1, ZRSR2) **or** mutated NRAS, KRAS, FLT3‑ITD or FLT3‑TKD. general_exclusion_criteria: History of gastrointestinal disorders impairing drug absorption (bowel obstruction, malabsorption, persistent nausea/vomiting/diarrhea > grade 1). Incomplete recovery from preceding chemotherapy toxicities greater than CTCAE grade 1 (except alopecia or grade 2 peripheral neuropathy). Presence of active systemic bacterial infection requiring IV antibiotics, invasive fungal infection, or clinically evident viral infection (HIV positivity, active hepatitis B or C). Recent major injury or surgery within 14 days prior to randomisation or anticipated major surgery during treatment. Unresolved adverse effects from prior radiotherapy (must recover from acute effects; a minimum 14‑day interval post‑radiotherapy). Concurrent second malignancy diagnosed ≤ 3 years ago, excluding adequately treated cervical carcinoma in situ, cutaneous basal cell carcinoma, or breast DCIS, or other tumors judged low‑risk by investigators. Active central nervous system involvement (brain metastasis) unless documented stability ≥ 8 weeks with definitive local therapy and off steroids for ≥ 1 month. Pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of effective contraception in women of child‑bearing potential; positive serum β‑hCG. Significant uncontrolled co‑morbidities such as interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis, resting hypoxia requiring supplemental oxygen, severe renal insufficiency (creatinine clearance < 30 mL/min), inflammatory bowel disease leading to baseline grade 2 or worse diarrhea. Requirement for concomitant potent CYP3A4 inducer or inhibitor therapy. History of serious cardiac events (syncope of cardiac cause, pathogenic ventricular arrhythmias, ventricular tachycardia/fibrillation, sudden cardiac death).

general_exclusion_criteria: History of significant cardiovascular disease (New York Heart Association Class II or greater heart failure, recent myocardial infarction, uncontrolled arrhythmias, left ventricular ejection fraction < 50%). Uncontrolled hypertension (blood pressure ≥ 140/90 mm Hg despite stable monotherapy). Active hemorrhage or high risk of gastrointestinal bleeding. Uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites. Symptomatic central nervous system metastases requiring intervention. Recent venous thromboembolism (deep-vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism) within six months. Interstitial lung disease or pulmonary fibrosis evident on baseline imaging. Persistent grade ≥ 2 adverse events attributable to prior therapy (excluding alopecia). Significant gastrointestinal pathology affecting oral drug uptake (malabsorption, chronic diarrhoea, bowel obstruction, total gastrectomy). Recent major (>6 weeks) or minor (<2 weeks) surgical procedure. Proteinuria ≥2+ on dipstick or ≥1 g/24 h. Serious active infection or documented viral hepatitis B/C (active) or human immunodeficiency virus infection. Cognitive or psychiatric disorder interfering with informed consent/compliance. Current illicit drug or alcohol dependence compromising protocol adherence. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, or unwillingness to employ adequate contraception.

Age range allowed: 18 years and older. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: solid tumour. Histology allowed: any solid‑tumour histology. general_exclusion_criteria:
Significant cardiovascular disease such as New York Heart Association Class II or higher, unstable angina, myocardial infarction within the preceding six months, or unstable arrhythmias within the preceding three months.
Primary central nervous system malignancy, untreated central nervous system metastases requiring anticancer therapy, or active central nervous system metastases.
Severe, uncontrolled systemic diseases—including clinically significant cardiovascular, pulmonary, or renal disorders—or ongoing infections.
History or complications of interstitial lung disease.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: gastric cancer involving gastroesophageal junction or upper portion of the stomach. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA (treatment‐naïve). Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: HER2‑positive.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Symptomatic central nervous system involvement or uncontrolled brain metastasis.
Carcinomatous meningitis or history thereof.
Active or ongoing serious infection requiring intravenous antibiotics.
Uncontrolled diabetes, hepatic, renal, or respiratory illness.
Severe or unstable angina, recent myocardial infarction or ischemia, symptomatic congestive heart failure, recent major arterial or venous thrombosis, clinically significant arrhythmias, NYHA Class II–IV heart disease within six months.
Uncontrolled hypertension.
Immunocompromised state, including documented HIV infection.
Known active hepatitis B or C infection.
Active or symptomatic pneumonitis, or history of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring corticosteroids.
Diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or suspicion thereof.
Any unresolved CTCAE Grade ≥2 adverse event attributable to prior therapy.
Resting electrocardiogram showing QTcF > 470 msec on two occasions within 24 hours or familial Long QT syndrome.
Concurrent use of potent cytochrome P450 3A4 inhibitors or inducers, or strong UDP‑glucuronosyltransferase 1A1 inhibitors.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: acute myeloid leukemia. Histology allowed: WHO 2022‐defined acute myeloid leukemia. Cancer burden allowed: Newly diagnosed, previously untreated, ineligible for intensive chemotherapy as defined by age ≥ 75 years or age 18‑74 years with at least one qualifying comorbidity (Eastern Cooperative Oncology Group performance status 2‑3, diffusion capacity of lungs for CO ≤65 %, forced expiratory volume in 1 second ≤65 %, baseline creatinine clearance <45 ml/min, total bilirubin >1.5 times upper limit of normal, or other severe medical condition deemed unsuitable for intensive chemotherapy). Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: Any prior antileukemic therapy for acute myeloid leukemia—including hypomethylating agents, venetoclax, tagraxofusp, purine analogues, cytarabine, intensive chemotherapy, chimeric antigen receptor‑T cell therapy, or other investigational agents. Biomarkers required: CD123‑positive and TP53‑mutated. Biomarkers excluded: TP53‑wild‑type.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of clinically active pneumonia or interstitial lung disease/pneumonitis. Uncontrolled cardiac insufficiency or recent myocardial infarction. Significant renal impairment (elevated serum creatinine outside acceptable limits). Clinically meaningful hepatic dysfunction (bilirubin markedly elevated, transaminases >5 times upper limit of normal). Active uncontrolled central nervous system metastasis or leptomeningeal disease. Known infection with human immunodeficiency virus, chronic hepatitis B or C requiring ongoing antiviral therapy. Poor functional status exceeding protocol-specified performance score thresholds. Concurrent participation in another therapeutic oncology trial. Pregnancy or breastfeeding.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥ 18 years. Sex allowed: both. Cancer type allowed: any solid tumor. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: advanced unresectable or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: NA. Prior treatment excluded: prior Farnesyl Transferase Inhibitor or HRAS inhibitor. Biomarkers required: HRAS mutation **or** HRAS amplification. Biomarkers excluded: NA
general_exclusion_criteria: Ongoing concurrent anti‑cancer therapy. Prior exposure to a Farnesyl Transferase Inhibitor or HRAS inhibitor. Major surgical procedure (other than minor/local) within 28 days before enrollment without full recovery. Presence of symptomatic central nervous system involvement—including spinal cord compression, leptomeningeal disease, or actively progressing brain metastasis. Prior treatment required: documented disease progression after having received, been intolerant to, been deemed ineligible for, or declined available standard anticancer therapies, including anti‑Programmed Death Receptor‑1/Programmed Death Ligand‑1 inhibitor or anti‑Cytotoxic T‑Ly​mphocyte Associated Antigen 4 antibody when applicable. general_exclusion_criteria: Any known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment, or history of other malignancy within two years. Residual toxicities from prior therapy not resolved to Grade 1 or less. Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic immunosuppressive corticosteroid therapy. Untreated or progressively worsening brain or central nervous system metastasis. History of organ transplantation, including allogenic hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation. Clinically significant or uncontrolled cardiac disease. Positive serologic testing for Hepatitis B virus, Hepatitis C virus, or Human Immunodeficiency Virus. Ongoing chronic systemic glucocorticoid administration exceeding ten milligrams daily of prednisone equivalents. Current acute infectious process necessitating systemic antibiotic, antifungal, or antiviral agents. Initiation of or modification to anticoagulant therapy within three months before first investigational drug dosing. Known medically serious uncontrolled conditions, specifically: vascular diseases such as vasculitis, arterial aneurysms, or other hemodynamically significant vascular anomalies; history of myocardial inflammation (myocarditis); gastrointestinal complications including bowel obstruction occurring within sixty days preceding Cycle 1 Day 1. Failure to meet predefined acceptable laboratory value thresholds.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Leiomyosarcoma. general_exclusion_criteria: History of myocardial infarction or unstable angina within past year; severe peripheral vascular disease; symptomatic arrhythmia requiring medication; left ventricular ejection fraction ≤50%; any immunodeficiency including HIV infection; chronic active hepatitis B or C (positive HBsAg + HBV DNA OR anti-HCV antibodies + HCV RNA); active uncontrolled infection; significant concurrent medical illness judged to increase risk; concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inducers within two weeks of starting study drug; inadequate bone marrow, renal, hepatic, or metabolic laboratory values outside stipulated ranges; pregnancy or breastfeeding; lack of adequate contraception for men with partner of child‑bearing potential or women of child‑bearing potential; inability to comply with study procedures or follow‑up; urgent need for rapid tumor reduction due to proximity to vital structures.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma. Prior treatment excluded: Use of any phosphoinositide 3‑kinase, AKT, or mechanistic target of rapamycin inhibitor; systemic non‑hormonal anti‑cancer therapy for locally advanced or metastatic HER2‑positive breast cancer administered before induction therapy. general_exclusion_criteria:
History or active inflammatory bowel disease.
Disease progression within six months of receiving any HER2‑directed therapy.
Type 2 diabetes requiring ongoing systemic treatment or any history of Type 1 diabetes.
Active hepatitis B virus infection.
Clinically significant and active liver disease—including severe hepatic impairment, viral hepatitis, alcoholic liver disease, or cirrhosis.
Symptomatic active lung disease, including pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.
Any history of leptomeningeal disease or carcinomatous meningitis.
Serious infection requiring intravenous antibiotics within seven days prior to enrollment.
Concurrent ocular or intraocular condition that would necessitate medical or surgical intervention to preserve vision.
Active inflammatory or infectious eye conditions or a history of idiopathic or autoimmune‑associated uveitis.

general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease, or evidence of pneumonitis/ILD on baseline imaging.
Uncontrolled intercurrent illness such as uncontrolled hypertension, active infection, symptomatic congestive heart failure, unstable angina, clinically significant cardiac arrhythmias, or serious psychiatric disorder limiting compliance.
QTcF greater than 470 milliseconds on screening electrocardiogram.
Past or current diagnosis of myelodysplastic syndrome or acute myeloid leukaemia, or features suggesting evolving MDS/AML.
Inability to swallow oral medication or gastrointestinal conditions expected to impair drug absorption (malabsorption syndromes, extensive gastric/bowel resection).
Recent major surgical procedures within 28 days prior to first dosing.
Known hypersensitivity to axatilimab, olaparib, or structurally related compounds.
Concurrent use of strong or moderate cytochrome P450 (especially CYP3A) inhibitors or inducers.
Active or untreated Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection (latent or active).

general_exclusion_criteria:
Known hypersensitivity or contraindication to REM‑422 components or related compounds.
Clinically significant active infection (excluding simple UTIs or mild bacterial pharyngitis responsive to treatment).
Active human immunodeficiency virus infection.
Currently active hepatitis B or hepatitis C infection.
Primary immunodeficiency disorders.
Requirement for daily systemic glucocorticoids ≥ 10 mg prednisolone equivalents.
Receipt of live vaccinations ≤ 6 weeks before first dose.
Use of strong cytochrome P450 3A inhibitors or inducers.
Concomitant use of potent gastric-acid reducing agents (e.g., PPIs, H₂ blockers) within 7 days of dosing.
Pregnancy, planned conception during study, or breastfeeding.
Malabsorptive syndromes, severe gastrointestinal pathology, or gastrectomy/bowel resections impairing drug uptake.
Prohibited concomitant medications taken ≤ 1 week before study start.
Significant cardiovascular disease deemed clinically meaningful.
Major surgery involving breach of a mesothelial surface or requiring general anaesthesia < 4 weeks ago.
History of solid-organ transplantation necessitating ongoing immunosuppression.
Autoimmune diseases (e.g., inflammatory bowel disease, rheumatic arthritides, systemic lupus erythematosus).
Recent therapeutic irradiation ≤ 7 days before initiating REM‑422.
Concurrent or preceding participation in another interventional oncology trial ≤ 3 weeks before entry.
Unwillingness or inability to adhere to protocol-specified assessments and visits.
Any medical condition judged by the investigators to compromise safety assessment or data integrity.

general_exclusion_criteria:
Has a known additional malignant disease, except if curatively treated with no evidence of recurrence for ≥3 years.  
Active central nervous system metastases not meeting stability criteria (clinical instability, new/enlarged lesions, leptomeningeal disease).  
Received a live or live‑attenuated vaccine within 30 days prior to first dose.  
Currently receiving chronic systemic glucocorticoids or other immunosuppressive therapy (excluding physiologic replacement dosing).  
Inhaled or topical steroids alone without active autoimmune disease are permitted.  
Active severe ocular disorders such as untreated keratitis or corneal ulceration (superficial punctate keratitis allowable if properly managed).  
Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment within the preceding 2 years (unless only replacement therapy).  
Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus.  
History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or currently active pneumonitis.  
Active infections (viral, bacterial, fungal) necessitating systemic antimicrobial therapy.  
Known infection with Human Immunodeficiency Virus.  
Chronic hepatitis B or hepatitis C viral infection.  
Major surgical procedure performed within 4 weeks prior to first dose.  
Allogeneic solid organ or hematopoietic stem cell transplantation.

Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Glioblastoma (World Health Organization grade 4 glioblastoma, including molecularly defined glioblastoma per 2021 WHO classification). Histology allowed: Glioblastoma. Cancer burden allowed: First documented recurrence of glioblastoma; tumor located in cerebral cortex/subcortical tissue (excludes lesions primarily in brainstem, spinal cord, multifocal involvement, bi‑hemispheric extension across corpus callosum, diffuse leptomeningeal dissemination, or extracranial disease). Prior treatment required: Completion of initial external‐beam radiation therapy (approximately 60 Gy in 30 fractions or equivalent) with or without concurrent temozolomide, and thereafter adjuvant temozolomide as administered per standard care; minimum interval of 12 weeks since completion of radiotherapy (patients <12 weeks eligible only with pathologic confirmation of recurrence or new distant enhancement); Karnofsky Performance Status ≥ 70; recovery to grade 0–1 toxicity from prior therapy. Prior treatment excluded: Any anticancer therapy for tumor recurrence other than surgical resection/debulkement; more than one episode of tumor progression/recurrence; investigational agents for newly diagnosed disease are permitted only if other criteria fulfilled. Biomarkers required: Positive HLA‑A*0201 genotype (tested locally at screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of pneumonitis; active autoimmune disease requiring systemic therapy within the past 2 years; immunodeficiency; known coagulopathy increasing bleeding risk or serious haemorrhage within 12 months; intratumoural/peritumoural haemorrhage higher than grade 1 persisting on serial imaging; gastrointestinal or other grade > 3 bleeding within 6 months; additional actively progressing malignancy or malignancy requiring systemic therapy within 1 year (surgical cure alone exempt); active infection requiring systemic antibiotics within 7 days; symptomatic central nervous system infection; HIV infection on antiretrovirals; active hepatitis B or C infection; recent (<3 months) acute pancreatitis; recent (>12 months) arterial thromboembolic event; recent (<12 months) clinically significant cardiovascular disease (myocardial infarction, unstable angina, stroke, TIA, CHF, uncontrolled arrhythmia, peripheral artery disease grade ≥ 2); active tuberculosis; known hypersensitivity to study product components; pregnancy, lactation, or intent to become pregnant up to 120 days after last dose; inability or unwillingness to use approved contraception (for females of child‑bearing potential and males with partners of child‑bearing potential); continuous systemic immunosuppressive therapy exceeding prednisolone ≤ 10 mg/day equivalence within 7 days; systemic immunosuppressive agents (methotrexate, azathioprine, etc.) within 6 months; high‑dose glucocorticoids (dex > 2 mg/day) within 2 weeks; dependence on therapeutic anticoagulation with warfarin at baseline; administration of any live vaccine within 30 days; severe psychiatric disorder or substance misuse interfering with trial adherence.

Medical contraindication to platinum‑based doublet chemotherapy.
Other primary malignant neoplasm unless cured ≥2 years ago, adequately resected non‑melanoma skin cancer, or in‑situ disease.
Persistent grade ≥2 toxicities attributable to previous anticancer therapy (excluding alopecia/vitlgo).
Grade >2 peripheral neuropathy (unless investigator deems appropriate).
Spinal cord compression unless asymptomatic and stable.
Significant cardiovascular disease (symptomatic CHF, unstable angina, serious arrhythmias, uncontrolled AFib, sustained VTach).
Concurrent receipt of other chemotherapy, investigational drugs, biological or hormonal cancer therapy.
Radiation therapy not meeting protocol allowances (must be >6 months old if definitive, limited palliative cranial or bone irradiation rules apply).
Untreated or progressive central nervous system metastasis; leptomeningeal carcinomatosis.
Positive tests for active Mycobacterium tuberculosis infection.
Active viral hepatitis (HBsAg-positive, HBV DNA–positive, HCV RNA‑positive, HDV coinfection) or unresolved hepatitis C/B infection.
Uncontrolled Human Immunodeficiency Virus infection.
Use of systemic immunosuppressive medications within 14 days (exceptions noted for low-dose steroids, intranasal/topical/local administration, pre‑medication dosing).
Receipt of live attenuated vaccination within 30 days.
Enrollment in another interventional clinical trial concurrently.
Known hypersensitivity to study drug components.
Pregnancy, lactation, or inadequate contraceptive measures for women of childbearing potential.

Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: All. Cancer type allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia. Histology allowed: chronic lymphocytic leukemia/small lymphocytic lymphoma. Prior treatment required: no prior CLL‑directed therapy; documentation of prior tetanus vaccination.
