Biomarkers required: IGHV unmutated status defined as <2 % divergence from germline sequence determined by molecular profiling. Biomarkers excluded: detection of del(17p) in ≥10 % of malignant cells; evidence of Richter transformation; serum immunoglobulin <400 mg/dL or necessity for chronic IVIG; active infection with HIV, hepatitis B virus, or hepatitis C virus; unresolved autoimmune cytopenia; prior exposure to anti‑PD‑1, anti‑PD‑L1, or anti‑PD‑L2 therapeutics.

* Significant organ dysfunction not meeting laboratory entry parameters (renal insufficiency, hepatic dysfunction, abnormal coagulation profile).

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: ≥18 years. Sex allowed: Both sexes. Cancer type allowed: Gastrointestinal stromal tumor. Histology allowed: Gastrointestinal stromal tumor. general_exclusion_criteria:
History of clinically significant cardiac disease including symptomatic arrhythmia requiring medication (other than β‑blocker or digoxin), congenital long QT syndrome, prolonged corrected QT interval (>470 ms), New York Heart Association class III–IV heart failure, recent myocardial infarction (<6 months), unstable angina, or recent coronary artery bypass graft/stent placement.
Arterial or venous thrombotic or embolic events (stroke/TIA, deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism) within six months preceding enrolment (unless appropriately treated and resolved over one month ago).
Uncontrolled or progressive central nervous system involvement, including untreated brain metastasis or leptomeningeal disease.
Active, uncontrolled systemic infectious processes (bacterial, fungal, viral) at screening.
Severe hepatic impairment (ALT/AST > 2.5× ULN without hepatic metastases or > 5× ULN with hepatic metastases) or total bilirubin > 1.5× ULN attributable to causes other than Gilberts’ disease.
Renal insufficiency with estimated glomerular filtration rate < 45 ml/min/1.73 m².
Pregnancy or breastfeeding.
Known positivity for Human Immunodeficiency Virus type 1 or 2.
Recent major surgery (within four weeks) or incomplete recovery from prior operative procedure.
Significant gastrointestinal disorders impairing oral intake or drug absorption (severe nausea/vomiting, extensive resections, malabsorptive syndromes, external biliary shunts).
Clinically meaningful ongoing grade ≥2 toxicities from prior anticancer therapy despite resolution to ≤grade 1 being required.
Any additional malignant neoplasm judged by the investigator to potentially confound safety or efficacy assessments.

Cancer burden allowed: No active central nervous system metastases; no progressing secondary malignant neoplasm. Prior treatment required: Receipt of pembrolizumab monotherapy, pembrolizumab‑based combination, or lenvatinib in a qualified parent study and currently receiving the same agent(s) at enrollment. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: NA. Biomarkers excluded: Presence of interstitial lung disease.
general_exclusion_criteria:
Severe hypersensitivity (grade ≥ 3) to pembrolizumab or its excipients. Recent live vaccination within 30 days prior to first dose of second course. Diagnosis of immunodeficiency or chronic systemic steroid/immunosuppressive therapy (>10 mg prednisolone equivalents) within 7 days of baseline. Known additional malignancy that is progressing or requires active treatment (exceptions noted). Active central nervous system metastases or leptomeningeal disease. Active autoimmune disease requiring systemic treatment in the preceding 2 years (physiological hormone replacements exempt). History of non‑infectious pneumonitis requiring steroids or current pneumonitis. Active infection necessitating systemic therapy. Documented HIV infection. Positive serology for hepatitis B or C (unless permissible per parent protocol). Pregnancy, lactation, or plans to become pregnant/father a child during study and for stipulated periods thereafter. Significant cardiovascular disease (arrhythmias, NYHA class III/IV heart failure, symptomatic ischemic heart disease). Hepatic decompensation (Child‑Pugh score > 6). Uncontrolled thyroid disorder. Uncontrolled diabetes mellitus. Previous allogenic tissue or solid‑organ transplantation. Current or historical active tuberculosis. 

Len​vatan ib‑specific exclusions: Major surgery within 3 weeks before first dose. Pre‑existent grade ≥ 3 gastrointestinal or nongastrointestinal fistulas. Urinary protein excretion ≥1 g/24 h. Left ventricular ejection fraction below institution’s normal limits. Radiographic involvement/invasion of major vessels or intratumoral cavitation. Corrected QT interval >480 ms. Clinically significant cardiovascular events within 12 months (NYHA class III/IV CHF, unstable angina, MI, cerebrovascular accident, serious arrhythmia). Conditions impairing oral absorption (malabsorption syndromes). Active hemoptysis within 3 weeks prior to first dose. Hypersensitivity to any lenvatinib component.

general_exclusion_criteria:
• Residual toxicity greater than Grade 1 from prior anticancer therapy (except alopecia or peripheral neuropathy).  
• Receipt of any other investigational agent concurrently.  
• Allergic reaction to components chemically related to study drugs.  
• Requirement for therapeutic‐dose anticoagulation.  
• Concomitant use of strong CYP3A4 inhibitors or inducers (unless stopped appropriately).  
• Medications that significantly prolong the QT interval.  
• Uncontrolled intercurrent acute illness.  
• Psychiatric disorder or social situation compromising adherence to study protocols.  
• Pregnancy or lactation.  
• Recent major cardiovascular events (stroke, myocardial infarction, unstable angina) within six months.  
• Conditions affecting gastrointestinal absorption (gastrectomy, intestinal bypass, severe diarrhoea, gastroparesis).  
• Retinal vein occlusion history.  
• Past or current pneumonitis or interstitial lung disease.

Histology allowed: Adenocarcinoma of the stomach or gastroesophageal junction. Cancer burden allowed: Metastatic or unresectable disease. Biomarkers required: HER2 overexpression (Immunohistochemistry score 3+) **or** HER2 amplification by in situ hybridisation or validated next‑generation sequencing **or** a qualified HER2 activating mutation (list as above). clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: >=18 years. Sex allowed: All sexes. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: Any histologically confirmed breast carcinoma. Cancer burden allowed: Locally advanced or metastatic disease (not amenable to curative surgery or radiotherapy), including measurable disease or bone‐only disease. Prior treatment required: No prior systemic anticancer therapy for locally advanced or metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior (neo)adjuvant endocrine therapy with disease recurrence during or within 12 months after the last dose; prior (neo)adjuvant cyclin-dependent kinase 4/6 inhibitor therapy with disease recurrence during or within 12 months after the last dose. Biomarkers required: (Estrogen Receptor–positive or Progesterone Receptor‑positive) and Human epidermal growth factor receptor 2‑negative. Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of visceral crisis causing imminent life‑threatening complication; current or past central nervous system metastases; inadequate renal function, hepatic dysfunction, or significant hematologic abnormality.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: NA. Sex allowed: Male and Female. Cancer type allowed: Cutaneous melanoma. Histology allowed: Non-blue-nevus, non-mucosal, non-acral lentiginous, non-uveal/ocular/choroidal melanoma. Cancer burden allowed: Unresectable and/or metastatic disease. Prior treatment required: Disease progression on anti‑programmed death 1 monoclonal antibody therapy (administered as monotherapy or in combination) AND measurable disease. Prior treatment excluded: NA. Biomarkers required: Presence of two functional copies of the G‑protein coupled estrogen receptor (GPER) protein‐coding sequence (assessed during screening). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria:
History of pneumonitis.
Heart failure.
Renal dysfunction.
Liver dysfunction.
Uncontrolled brain metastases.
HIV infection or active viral hepatitis.
Performance status worse than ECOG ≥ 2.
Allogeneic solid organ or bone marrow transplantation.
Active unstable autoimmune disease or significant immunodeficiency.
Recent anticancer/investigational agent exposure within four weeks of first dose (other than permitted washout).
Radiation therapy within two weeks of initiating study medication.
Concurrent serious medical conditions making participation unsafe.
Previous severe hypersensitivity reaction to anti‑PD‑1 therapy precluding further pembrolizumab administration.

clinical_space_summary: Age range allowed: > 18 years. Sex allowed: Male and female. Cancer type allowed: Breast cancer. Histology allowed: NA. Cancer burden allowed: Advanced or metastatic disease (must be evaluable disease with measurable lesion per RECIST 1.1 or predominantly bone disease amenable to imaging). Prior treatment required: No more than 1 prior hormonal regimen for advanced/metastatic disease (hormone combinations containing CDK4/6 inhibitors are permitted); No more than 1 prior chemotherapy (including antibody–drug conjugates) for advanced/metastatic disease. Prior treatment excluded: Prior exposure to OP‑1250. Biomarkers required: Estrogen receptor positive **AND** HER2 negative (determined on latest available archival metastatic tumor tissue). Biomarkers excluded: NA.
general_exclusion_criteria: History of another malignant neoplasm likely to interfere with safety or efficacy assessments.; Clinically significant uncontrolled heart disease or cardiac repolarisation abnormalities.; Cerebrovascular accident occurring within six months before first study dosing.; Pulmonary embolism or deep vein thrombosis within six months preceding enrollment, or investigators deem the participant at heightened thrombotic risk.; Past or existing pneumonitis/interstitial lung disease.; Presence of leptomeningeal disease or spinal cord compression.; Gastrointestinal diseases affecting oral drug absorption.; Documented human immunodeficiency virus infection.; Significant chronic liver disease classified as Child‑Pugh class B or C, including active viral hepatitis (hepatitis B or C), alcoholic liver disease, or established cirrhosis.; Severe dermatologic adverse events such as Stevens‑Johnson syndrome, toxic epidermal necrolysis, erythrema multiforme, or DRESS syndrome.; Ongoing acute infection or circumstances placing the individual at high risk for serious infection (immunodeficiency, poorly controlled diabetes, unstable cardiovascular illness, frailty, inadequate nutrition).; Any major concomitant medical, psychiatric, or social condition compromising protocol compliance or data interpretation.
